functions.name: ColorPanel.Choose
functions.description: Shows color chooser. If you pass a color, it's preselected. Versions until 8.5 return OK when cancel was selected. This was corrected for 9.0. Windows may not select the given color if it doesn't like it.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: The color to prefill.

parameters.name: Expand
parameters.description: Whether to show dialog expanded. Default is 0 to show collapsed. Pass 1 for expanded view.

examples.label: Choose color:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "ColorPanel.Choose"; "#FF0000")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.Choose"; "#FF0000")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ColorPanel.GetAlpha
functions.description: Queries alpha component of current color. Alpha comes as floating point value between 0.0 and 1.0.

functions.name: ColorPanel.GetColor
functions.description: Queries current color. Returns color as hex values, e.g. FF0000 for red.

parameters.name: ReturnAsNumbers
parameters.description: Pass 1 to return color as list of red, green and blue, e.g. "123¶234¶56" Pass 0 (default) for return as hex value, e.g. "7788FF".

examples.label: Query color:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$c
; Value:
MBS
( "ColorPanel.GetColor" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 ]
Set Field [
Color Panel::Color
; "#" &
$c
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.GetColor" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$c</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>"#" & $c </Calculation>
    <Field table="Color Panel" id="99999" name="Color "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ColorPanel.GetColorChangedEvaluate
functions.description: Queries expression.

functions.name: ColorPanel.GetColorChangedScript
functions.description: Queries script to run when color changed.

functions.name: ColorPanel.GetColorName
functions.description: Queries catalog name for current color. If the color is defined via color catalog, you may get the name here, e.g. labelColor.

functions.name: ColorPanel.GetVisible
functions.description: Queries visible state. Returns 1 if visible or 0 if not.

functions.name: ColorPanel.SetAlpha
functions.description: Sets alpha part of the color.

parameters.name: Alpha
parameters.description: The new alpha value. Range from 0.0 to 1.0.

examples.label: Set Alpha to 50%:

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "ColorPanel.SetAlpha";
0.5
) ]
examples.xml_code:

      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.SetAlpha"; 0.5 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>

functions.name: ColorPanel.SetColor
functions.description: Sets color.

parameters.name: Color
parameters.description: A color value. This can be a common color name or color in hex notation: # followed by 2 digits for red, green, blue and optional alpha, e.g. #FF0000 for full red.

examples.label: Set red:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "ColorPanel.SetColor"; "#FF0000" )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.SetColor"; "#FF0000" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ColorPanel.SetColorChangedEvaluate
functions.description: Sets expression to run when color changed. The plugin can evaluate this expression to set variables, call plugin functions to trigger other things. Use ColorPanel.GetColor to query new color. It may be good to call this with empty expression when you leave the layout where you need it to avoid unexpected calculations later.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to calculate.

functions.name: ColorPanel.SetColorChangedScript
functions.description: Sets script to trigger if color changed. You may need to clear this to disable the plugin from triggering script while in layout mode until version 8.4, where we don't call trigger in layout mode. It may be good to call this with empty script and file name when you leave the layout where you need it to avoid unexpected script triggers later.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: Script parameter to pass.

examples.label: Set color changed script trigger:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "ColorPanel.SetColorChangedScript"; Get(FileName); "ColorChanged" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.SetColorChangedScript"; Get(FileName); "ColorChanged" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Clear script trigger:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "ColorPanel.SetColorChangedScript"; ""; "")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.SetColorChangedScript"; ""; "")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ColorPanel.SetVisible
functions.description: Shows or hides the panel.

parameters.name: Visible
parameters.description: Pass 1 to show panel or 0 to hide.

functions.name: ColorPanel.Show
functions.description: Shows color panel.

examples.label: Show panel and optionally load a color:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
ColorPanel.SetColorChangedScript
"; Get(FileName); "ColorChanged" ) ]
If [ Length (
Color Panel::Color
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
ColorPanel.SetColor
";
Color Panel::Color
) ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "ColorPanel.Show" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.SetColorChangedScript"; Get(FileName); "ColorChanged" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( Color Panel::Color ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.SetColor"; Color Panel::Color ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ColorPanel.Show" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.AddDirectory
functions.description: Adds a directory to watch. With the actions you can define what changes to report.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The directory path to verify.

parameters.name: Recursive
parameters.description: Whether to recursively check subdirectories. Pass 1 to turn on or 0 to turn off.

parameters.name: Action
parameters.description: The changes to monitor.

examples.label: Watch for changes in directory:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("DirectoryWatcher.AddDirectory"; $Watcher; Directory Watcher::Directory; 1; "Filename¶DirName¶Attributes¶Size¶LastWrite¶LastAccess¶Creation¶Security")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.ChangeCount
functions.description: Queries the change count. We queue the changes in a list and you can query it.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.Create
functions.description: Creates a new directory watcher. Works on server and client. May not work for network shared disks as most drivers don't pass through change events.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.GetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries the expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.GetFileName
functions.description: Returns the file name for the watcher.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.GetScriptName
functions.description: Returns the script name for the watcher.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.NextChange
functions.description: Queries next change in queue. The list contains first the path name, second the action code and third the name of the action.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.Release
functions.description: Releases directory watcher. This will free all memory used and stops the checks.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.ReleaseAll
functions.description: Releases all directory watchers. Be aware that on a server this may free objects, which are used by other scripts running in parallel!

examples.label: Cleanup:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DirectoryWatcher.ReleaseAll" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DirectoryWatcher.ReleaseAll" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.SetEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to evaluate.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.SetFileName
functions.description: Sets the file name for watcher. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.SetScriptName
functions.description: Sets the script name for watcher. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: Watcher
parameters.description: The watcher reference number.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

functions.name: DirectoryWatcher.TotalChangeCount
functions.description: Queries the total number of changes seen. Good for debugging to see how many changes happened between two time stamps by comparing the counter before and after.

functions.name: DocumentCameraScan.Close
functions.description: Closes the scan dialog. If the dialog is not open, this function does nothing. The plugin will close it automatically when the script is triggered.

functions.name: DocumentCameraScan.ErrorMessage
functions.description: Queries error message.

examples.label: Script may look like this:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$param
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
If [
$param
= "Error" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to scan" ;
MBS
( "DocumentCameraScan.ErrorMessage" ) ]
Else If [
$param
= "Scan" ]
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Import” ; Parameter:    ]
Else If [
$param
= "Cancel" ]
# do nothing
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$param</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $param = "Error" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to scan" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentCameraScan.ErrorMessage" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $param = "Scan" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="Import"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $param = "Cancel" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> do nothing</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DocumentCameraScan.Initialize
functions.description: Initializes the document camera scan functions. Requires iOS 13 or newer.

examples.label: Script to initialize:
examples.text_code:
# Initialize document camera scanner
#
If [
MBS
("
DocumentCameraScan.IsInitialized
") = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("DocumentCameraScan.Initialize") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to initialize" ;
$r
]
Else If [
MBS
("
DocumentCameraScan.Supported
") = 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Not supported on this device." ]
End If
End If

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to initialize" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$r </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>

functions.name: DocumentCameraScan.PageCount
functions.description: Queries page count.

examples.label: Import scan result:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "DocumentCameraScan.PageCount" ) ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
# store image
New Record/Request
Set Field [
Contacts::Video
;
MBS
( "
DocumentCameraScan.PageImage
";
$Index
; "PNG"; "test.png") ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: On ]
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentCameraScan.PageCount" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentCameraScan.PageImage"; $Index; "PNG"; "test.png") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Contacts" id="99999" name="Video "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DocumentCameraScan.PageImage
functions.description: Queries a page as image. See DocumentCameraScan.PageCount for the count of pages. You may use PDFKit functions to create a PDF document if needed.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the page. Range is 0 to DocumentCameraScan.PageCount -1.

parameters.name: ImageType
parameters.description: The type of image to return. Either JPEG or PNG. Default is JPEG.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The filename to use for the picture. Default is "image" with the extension matching the image type.

examples.label: Import scan result:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
DocumentCameraScan.PageCount
" ) ]
If [
$count
> 0 ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
Loop
# store image
New Record/Request
Set Field [
Contacts::Video
;
MBS
( "DocumentCameraScan.PageImage";
$Index
; "PNG"; "test.png") ]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: On ]
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentCameraScan.PageCount" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $count > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store image</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentCameraScan.PageImage"; $Index; "PNG"; "test.png") </Calculation>
    <Field table="Contacts" id="99999" name="Video "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="False"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DocumentCameraScan.Scan
functions.description: Show the dialog to scan. Please use DocumentCameraScan.Initialize before and check DocumentCameraScan.Supported to make sure you only show the button when it may work.

examples.label: Start the dialog:

Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "DocumentCameraScan.Scan" ) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to scan" ;
$r
]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentCameraScan.Scan" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: DocumentCameraScan.SetScript
functions.description: Sets the script to trigger when scan is done. Parameter to the script is either Cancel, Error or Scan. In case of error, please use DocumentCameraScan.ErrorMessage to query the error message to display. When you get Scan as parameter, you may use DocumentCameraScan.PageCount function to query number of pages scanned. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name where the script is located.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name.

examples.label: Script may look like this:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$param
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
If [
$param
= "Error" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to scan" ;
MBS
( "
DocumentCameraScan.ErrorMessage
" ) ]
Else If [
$param
= "Scan" ]
Perform Script [ Specified: From list ; “Import” ; Parameter:    ]
Else If [
$param
= "Cancel" ]
# do nothing
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$param</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $param = "Error" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Failed to scan" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>MBS( "DocumentCameraScan.ErrorMessage" ) </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $param = "Scan" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>  </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="Import"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="125" name="Else If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $param = "Cancel" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> do nothing</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.AddErrorMessage
functions.description: Adds a custom error to the error list used by the plugin. This is used for FM.ErrorMessage function and our trace logging. The new errors are only stored in memory, so you need to add them each time in the start script, e.g. from reading a table. The MBS Plugin has a list of over 260 error messages hard coded. But since newer FileMaker versions introduce new errors, you can add newer ones yourself here. Feel free to let us know newer error codes to add to the plugin.

parameters.name: ErrorNumber
parameters.description: The error number.

parameters.name: ErrorMessage
parameters.description: The error message to use.

examples.label: Add a test error and query it:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.AddErrorMessage"; 12345; "Test error" ) & ": " &
MBS
("
FM.ErrorMessage
"; 12345)
Example result:
OK: Test error
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.AddErrorMessage"; 12345; "Test error" ) & ": " &
MBS("FM.ErrorMessage"; 12345)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.AllowFileDragDrop
functions.description: Installs fix for file drag and drop problem. When we have a container in FileMaker with e.g. a zip file and drag the file content to the Finder, nothing happens. This plugin functions installs some code which intercepts those file drags and makes them work. Internally the file data is obtained and written to temporary folder. The finder can copy the file there and our plugin can later delete the temp file. Works better with non interactive containers. This does not change drag & drop of images. With plugin version 7.1, PDF drag and drop is also supported.

parameters.name: Enable
parameters.description: Whether to enable or disable. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable. Default if no parameter is given is enable.

parameters.name: TempFolder
parameters.description: The native path of temp folder to use. Leave empty for standard one.

parameters.name: DeleteTempFiles
parameters.description: Whether to delete temp files. Default is 1 to delete last file them when next file is to be dragged.

examples.label: Allow file drops from container to Finder:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.AllowFileDragDrop" )]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.AllowFileDragDrop" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.Argument
functions.description: Query an argument passed to FileMaker from command line. You can pass arguments to FileMaker when launching it from command line. Or on Windows pass them from a link to FileMaker on desktop or in the start menu. This way you can query argument from your scripts and react on them, e.g. decide to set more buttons visible for a debug or advanced mode. The arguments can include on Windows file path to a database which was opened to launch FileMaker.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: The index of the argument to retrieve. From 0 to FM.ArgumentCount -1.

examples.label: Query all arguments:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While(
// setup 
[count = MBS( "FM.ArgumentCount" ); index = 0; list = ""]; 
// condition
index < count ; 
// add nth argument to list
[ list = list & MBS( "FM.Argument"; index ) & ¶; index = index + 1 ];
// return result
list )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.ArgumentCount

// setup 
[count = MBS( "FM.ArgumentCount" ); index = 0; list = ""]; 
// condition
index < count ; 
// add nth argument to list
[ list = list & MBS( "FM.Argument"; index ) & ¶; index = index + 1 ];
// return result
list )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.CF
functions.description: Calls a custom function given the name with FileMaker values. This allows you to store expressions for custom functions in a MBS dictionary, so you can refer to them by name. Parameters are passed to the function preserving the data type. Please pass as many parameters as needed. If the function needs more parameter, undefined values are used to fill up. If the function needs less parameters, additional ones are dropped. There is no limit in the number of parameters for MBS Plugin here. Recursion is limited by FileMaker with about 200 deep. The plugin has a hard limit of 50000 for recursion, but before that, the FileMaker engine will report running out of memory. The dictionary must contain the given Name as key and as value a valid FileMaker expression. This expression may have lines on the beginning with /// to name variables, one line per variable name. The expression to evaluate can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server it must always be in english. Two special variables are always defined: FunctionName and DictionaryRef with the values filled with the parameters you passed above. If you like to have a function with variable parameters, you can use ParameterIndex and ParameterCount in your calculation. To query plugin parameter values, we have MBS("FM. CF .Parameter"; index) to query them. See also CF function for shorter access. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Parameters parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: DictionaryRef
parameters.description: The reference number to the dictionary. We prefer to use Dictionary.CreateNamed to create a special dictionary named " CF " and refer it globally with that name.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name of the function.

parameters.name: Parameters...
parameters.description: Value to pass as parameter.

examples.label: Call Concat function with two parameters:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.
CF
"; "
CF
"; "Concat"; "Hello"; "World")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.CF"; "CF"; "Concat"; "Hello"; "World")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Call a function and pass field value, calculation and hard coded value:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.
CF
"; "
CF
"; "Test";
MyTable::MyField
; Get(AccountName); "Hello"; Round(
$Total
+ (
$Total
*
$taxRate
); 2) )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.CF"; "CF"; "Test"; MyTable::MyField; Get(AccountName); "Hello"; Round($Total + ($Total * $taxRate); 2) )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create dictionary, add a function and
examples.text_code:
# create environment
Set Variable [
$
CF
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.CreateNamed
"; "test" ) ]
#
# Register one function. Replacing older one with same name
Set Variable [
$FunctionName
; Value: "Greet" ]
Set Variable [
$Body
; Value: "/// Name¶¶\"Welcome \" & Name & \"!\"" ]

$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Dictionary.SetValueForKey
"; "test";
$FunctionName
;
$Body
) ]
#
# now run it
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("FM.
CF
"; "test"; "Greet"; "Peter") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Result" ;
$r
]
#
# cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
Dictionary.Release
"; "test") ]

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Greet" </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>"/// Name¶¶\"Welcome \" & Name & \"!\"" </Calculation>
    </Value>

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

      <Calculation>MBS("FM.CF"; "test"; "Greet"; "Peter") </Calculation>

  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>

      </Button>

        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> cleanup</Text>
  </Step>

functions.name: FM.CompareTables
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.CompareTables"; FileName1; TableName1; IDField1; FileName2; TableName2; IDField2 { ; Options } )
functions.description: Compare two tables. We compare the field names for both tables and report added, removed and common field names. Then we check the record identifiers to report added, changed and common IDs. If IDField1 and IDField2 names are the same in both tables, we compare the common records to report the changed record identifiers. Does not compare the internal FileMaker row IDs. Records are compared in 100 records block to limit memory usage.

parameters.name: FileName1
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files. File extension is optional for file name.

parameters.name: TableName1
parameters.description: The name of the first table (newer). Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: IDField1
parameters.description: The name of the ID field in first table. Can be a text or numeric field.

parameters.name: FileName2
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: TableName2
parameters.description: The name of the second table (older). Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: IDField2
parameters.description: The name of the ID field in second table. Can be a text or numeric field.

parameters.name: Options
parameters.description: Pass options. Add 1 to include details. Add 2 to include all the IDs found in both.

functions.name: FM.DeleteRecord
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.DeleteRecord"; FileName; TableName; IDField; IDValue { ; IDField2; IDValue2; IDField3; IDValue3 } )
functions.description: Deletes an existing record in a table in one line. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must be unique. Please note that FM.DeleteRecord and FM.DeleteRecords are nearly identical. Just FM.DeleteRecord provides the check to make sure only one record matches while FM.DeleteRecords allows several records to be deleted. So with FM.DeleteRecords you can easily erase a lot of data if you pass wrong ID! With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Version 8.2 allows to specify up to 3 ID parameters. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. The SQL functions in FileMaker do not trigger OnWindowsTransaction script trigger.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to delete record from. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: IDField
parameters.description: The name of the first field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue
parameters.description: The value for the first ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

parameters.name: IDField2
parameters.description: The name of the second field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue2
parameters.description: The value for second ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

parameters.name: IDField3
parameters.description: The name of the third field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue3
parameters.description: The value for third ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

functions.name: FM.DeleteRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.DeleteRecords"; FileName; TableName; IDField; IDValue { ; IDField2; IDValue2; IDField3; IDValue3 } )
functions.description: Deletes existing records in a table in one line. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must not be unique, so several records can be updated at once. Please note that FM.DeleteRecord and FM.DeleteRecords are nearly identical. Just FM.DeleteRecord provides the check to make sure only one record matches while FM.DeleteRecords allows several records to be deleted. So with FM.DeleteRecords you can easily erase a lot of data if you pass wrong ID! With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. Version 8.2 allows to specify up to 3 ID parameters. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to delete records from. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: IDField
parameters.description: The name of the field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue
parameters.description: The value for the ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

parameters.name: IDField2
parameters.description: The name of the second field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue2
parameters.description: The value for second ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

parameters.name: IDField3
parameters.description: The name of the third field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue3
parameters.description: The value for third ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

functions.name: FM.DisableMouseWheel
functions.description: Disables use of mouse wheel on Windows. Some clients prefer to disable mouse wheel to not go from record to record while editing a record. This is no longer needed for FileMaker 15.

functions.name: FM.EnableMouseWheel
functions.description: Enables use of mouse wheel on Windows.

functions.name: FM.ErrorMessage
functions.description: Queries an error message for a FileMaker error. Our plugin uses an internal table to get meaningful text messages for an error code. Returns empty text for unknown errors. Error code zero usually means okay. The MBS Plugin has a list of over 260 error messages hard coded. But since newer FileMaker versions introduce new errors, you can add newer ones yourself using FM.AddErrorMessage function.

parameters.name: ErrorCode
parameters.description: The error code from FileMaker, e.g. Evaluate()

functions.name: FM.Evaluate
functions.description: Similar to FileMaker's built-in Evaluate. This call gets logged in trace.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

examples.label: Evaluate an expression:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.Evaluate"; "1+2" )

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.ExecuteFileSQL
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; FileName; SQL Statement { ; Column Delimiter; Record Delimiter; Params... } )
functions.description: Execute the SQL Statement against a FileMaker database. This function requires FileMaker 11 or newer. Please use FM.ExecuteSQL on FileMaker 10 or older. There must be an occurrence of the Table on the current tables' Graph in the file you specified. If filename is empty, you use current file. SELECT returns the the records in as a tabbed delimited text string. All other SQL commands return empty text. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to check for errors. Supported Commands: SELECT UPDATE (see limitation below) DELETE INSERT CREATE TABLE DROP TABLE CREATE INDEX DROP INDEX WHERE String comparisons are Case Sensitive! WHERE First_Name="todd" will not find records that have "Todd" in the first Name Field. You can wrap the field you are searching for with "lower" like this WHERE lower(First_Name)="todd" KNOWN LIMITATIONS Schema editing Record Locking Errors are Not Reported! This means you must exercise caution when using UPDATE. CAUTION! This is a very powerful function! You can put field names in double quotes and string values in single quotes. Use FM.ExecuteSQLOnIdle for special commands which change database like ALTER TABLE. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Error 8309 means you did pass wrong data type, like string for a number. You can make queries against FILEMAKER_TABLES and FILEMAKER_FIELDS to query information about fields in your solution. The functions FM.QueryBaseTableNames , FM.QueryFieldsForBaseTableName , FM.QueryFieldsForTableName and FM.QueryTableNames provide simplified functions for some queries. FILEMAKER_TABLES and FILEMAKER_BASETABLES tables contains: 1. TableName 2. TableID 3. BaseTableName 4. BaseFileName 5. ModCount FILEMAKER_FIELDS table contains: 1. TableName 2. FieldName 3. FieldType (the SQL data type, not the FileMaker data type) 4. FieldID 5. FieldClass (Normal, Summary, Calculated) 6. FieldReps 7. ModCount Some standard SQL functions work in FileMaker including CHR, COALESCE, LEFT, LENGTH,  LOWER, LTRIM, MID, RIGHT, RTRIM,  SPACE, SUBSTR, SUBSTRING, TRIM and UPPER. There may be more! If you need longer delimiters, please use FM.SQL.Execute with FM.SQL.Text to use multiple characters for column and record delimiters. Does not take part in transactions in FileMaker. Changes are made directly to the database, so you can't revert them later with Revert Transaction script step. For SELECT COUNT(*) you better use FM.ExecuteFileSQLValue as it returns the number as number. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: Column Delimiter
parameters.description: Columns Separator. Default is tab character, which has unicode code point 9. You can pass delimiter as text (one character only) or as unicode code point (as number).

parameters.name: Record Delimiter
parameters.description: Record Separator. Default is return character, which has unicode code point 13. You can pass delimiter as text or as unicode code point.

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.result: Returns result of SQL expression or error.

$FirstName

$LastName
;

;

;

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">

    </Value>

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$LastName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Street test"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Street</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Zip test"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Zip</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"City test"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$City</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Country test"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Country</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>"Phone test"</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Phone</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; "INSERT INTO \"Export records\" (\"First Name\", \"Last Name\", \"Street\", \"Zip\", \"City\", \"Country\", \"Phone\") VALUES (?,?,?,?,?,?,?)"; 9; 13; $FirstName; $LastName; $Street; $Zip; $City; $City; $Country; $Phone</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage" )</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set timestamp field:
examples.text_code:
# via CURRENT_TIMESTAMP constant:
Set Variable [
$r1
; Value:
MBS
("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; "UPDATE Kontakte SET Datum = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ")]
# or cast text to timestamp:
Set Variable [
$r2
; Value:
MBS
("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; "UPDATE Kontakte SET Datum = CAST('2017-04-12 14:14:14' AS TIMESTAMP) ")]
# via CURTIMESTAMP constant:
Set Variable [
$r3
; Value:
MBS
("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; "UPDATE Kontakte SET Datum = CURTIMESTAMP ")]
# or to now plus one hour:
Set Variable [
$r4
; Value:
MBS
("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; "UPDATE Kontakte SET Datum = CURTIMESTAMP + 3600 ")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> via CURRENT_TIMESTAMP constant:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; "UPDATE Kontakte SET Datum = CURRENT_TIMESTAMP ")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> or cast text to timestamp:</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS ("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; "UPDATE Kontakte SET Datum = CAST('2017-04-12 14:14:14' AS TIMESTAMP) ")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.ExecuteFileSQLOnIdle
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.ExecuteFileSQLOnIdle"; SQL Statement; FileName { ; Column Delimiter; Record Delimiter; Params... } )
functions.description: Executes a SQL command at idle time. This is similar to FM.ExecuteFileSQL , except that it waits until idle to execute.. You run a script pause after using this function to give it time to execute. See the other function for more info. Seems like on FileMaker 11 you get LastError 8310 if you use fields with underscore in the name. You avoid that by placing the field names in quotes. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. You can do more schema changes via ODATA interface on FileMaker Server including changing or renaming fields. Please notice that FM.ExecuteFileSQLOnIdle and FM.ExecuteFileSQL have the filename parameter on different positions. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: the SQL statement

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of the database. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: Column Delimiter
parameters.description: Columns Separator. Default is tab character, which has unicode code point 9. You can pass delimiter as text (one character only) or as unicode code point (as number).

parameters.name: Record Delimiter
parameters.description: Record Separator. Default is return character, which has unicode code point 13. You can pass delimiter as text (one character only) or as unicode code point (as number).

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

examples.label: Create in a script and check error:
examples.text_code:
# set command to run
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("FM.ExecuteFileSQLOnIdle"; "ALTER TABLE Kontakte ADD MyField varchar"; "Kontakte.fmp12") ]
# Make pause to run it
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
# Check error status
Set Variable [
$errorNumber
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$errorMessage
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage
" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> set command to run</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.ExecuteFileSQLOnIdle"; "ALTER TABLE Kontakte ADD MyField varchar"; "Kontakte.fmp12") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Make pause to run it</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="62" name="Pause/Resume Script">
    <PauseTime value="ForDuration"/>
    <Calculation>,1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Check error status</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$errorNumber</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$errorMessage</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Create a field:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("FM.ExecuteFileSQLOnIdle";"ALTER TABLE TestTable ADD MyField varchar"; Get(FileName)) ]
Pause/Resume Script [ Duration (seconds): ,1 ]
If [
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError
")  ≠ 0 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Failed to add field" ;
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError
") & ": " &
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage
") ]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Added field." ; "OK" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>

functions.name: FM.ExecuteFileSQLValue
functions.description: Execute the SQL Statement against a FileMaker database. Just like FM.ExecuteFileSQL , but only returns first value of first row in result. This preserves the value type without conversion to text. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.name: FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError
functions.description: Queries the last error code from an SQL call Set by FM.InsertRecord , FM.SQL.Execute , FM.ExecuteSQL , FM.ExecuteFileSQL or FM.ExecuteSQLOnIdle . The Errors number s are the same ones as the ones retuned by Native FileMaker function "Get(LastError)" Examples: 1200 - Generic Calculation Error" 200 - "Access Denied" 106 - "Missing Table" Known Limitations: Record Locking Errors are Not Reported! This means you must exercise caution when using UPDATE. There is no way to determine if an update actually occurs unless you check the value again after the update occured. In general error 8309 is a semantic error, so something is wrong in your SQL. 8310 is a syntax error, something in the SQL is not correctly parsed. Error 8309 means you did pass wrong data type, like string for a number. See also FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage .

examples.label: Get Last SQL Error
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage
functions.description: Queries the last error message from an SQL call Set by FM.InsertRecord , FM.SQL.Execute , FM.ExecuteSQL , FM.ExecuteFileSQL , FM.ExecuteSQLOnIdle and other SQL using functions. If last error code was 0 (no error), you get an empty string. Error may be something like this: ERROR: FQL0007/(1:7): The column named "fieldname" does not exist in any table in the column reference's scope.

parameters.name: FQL0001
parameters.description: There is an error in the syntax of the query.

parameters.name: FQL0002
parameters.description: The table named "?0" does not exist.

parameters.name: FQL0003
parameters.description: The table named "?0" already exists in this query.

parameters.name: FQL0004
parameters.description: The query is too complex. The maximum number of tables has been exceeded.

parameters.name: FQL0005
parameters.description: Expressions involving aggregations are not supported.

parameters.name: FQL0006
parameters.description: "The column named "?0" appears in more than one table in the column reference's scope."

parameters.name: FQL0007
parameters.description: The column named "?0" does not exist in any table in the column reference's scope.

parameters.name: FQL0008
parameters.description: The table named "?0" does not exist in the column reference's scope.

parameters.name: FQL0009
parameters.description: The column named "?1" does not exist in table "?0".

parameters.name: FQL0010
parameters.description: The literal value "?0" is not a valid DATE, TIME or TIMESTAMP.

parameters.name: FQL0011
parameters.description: Predicate must contain a logical operation (=, <, OR, AND, IS NULL, ...).

parameters.name: FQL0012
parameters.description: The ordinal reference "?0" in the ORDER BY clause is not valid.

parameters.name: FQL0013
parameters.description: Incompatible types in assignment.

parameters.name: FQL0014
parameters.description: The number of values in a VALUES row value constructor does not match the number of values in the target.

parameters.name: FQL0015
parameters.description: The number of values in an INSERT...SELECT statement does not match the number of values in the target.

parameters.name: FQL0016
parameters.description: A subquery contains an illegal outer reference to a column in the INSERT's target table.

parameters.name: FQL0017
parameters.description: An expression contains data types that cannot be compared.

parameters.name: FQL0018
parameters.description: An expression contains incompatible data types.

parameters.name: FQL0019
parameters.description: The result data type of a CASE expression cannot be inferred; they are all NULL.

parameters.name: FQL0020
parameters.description: An invalid number of parameters was supplied to the function "?0"

parameters.name: FQL0021
parameters.description: Parameter number ?0 to the function "?1" is not of the correct type.

parameters.name: FQL0022
parameters.description: A subquery expression must have exactly one value in the SELECT list.

parameters.name: FQL0023
parameters.description: A CAST expression requested an invalid data type conversion.

parameters.name: FQL0024
parameters.description: A reference to ROWID must be qualified if more than one table is present in the query.

parameters.name: FQL0025
parameters.description: All non-aggregated column references in the SELECT list and HAVING clause must be in the GROUP BY clause.

parameters.name: FQL0026
parameters.description: The number of columns in both inputs to a UNION operation must be the same.

parameters.name: FQL0027
parameters.description: The data types of corresponding columns in the inputs to a UNION operation must be the same.

parameters.name: FQL0028
parameters.description: Field repetitions must be numeric and between 1 and ?0.

parameters.name: FQL0029
parameters.description: A field repetition in the SET clause of an UPDATE statement must be a constant.

parameters.name: FQL0030
parameters.description: "?0" is an invalid function.

parameters.name: FQL0031
parameters.description: The the parameter's type cannot be inferred in this context. At least one query parameter must be an expression, a column or a constant.

parameters.name: FQL0032
parameters.description: A query may contain either named parameters or dynamic parameters, but not both.

parameters.name: FQL0033
parameters.description: Column names in FROM clause subqueries must be unique.

parameters.name: FQL0034
parameters.description: The number of output columns in a FROM clause subquery must match the number of columns in the table's name list.

parameters.name: FQL0035
parameters.description: Cursor support is not enabled for this query.

parameters.name: FQL0036
parameters.description: A cursor with the name "?0" already exists.

parameters.name: FQL0037
parameters.description: There is no cursor with the name "?0".

parameters.name: FQL0038
parameters.description: The cursor "?0" is already open.

parameters.name: FQL0039
parameters.description: The cursor "?0" is not open.

parameters.name: FQL0040
parameters.description: The target cursor "?0" does not reference a query that is valid for WHERE CURRENT OF .

parameters.name: FQL0041
parameters.description: The target cursor "?0" does not reference the same table as the current statement.

parameters.name: FQL0042
parameters.description: The default value for column "?0" does not match the column's data type.

parameters.name: FQL0043
parameters.description: The string "?0" is not a valid stream name.

parameters.name: FQL0044
parameters.description: The column "?0" is not valid in this context. The targets of GETAS and PUTAS must be Container fields.

examples.label: Custom function to log the last SQL error to a log file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
	[
		e = MBS( "IsError" ) ;
		r = If(
			e ;
			Let(
				[
					errorCode = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError" ) ;
					errorText = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage" ) ;
					sql = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL" ) ;
					path = "/Users/cs/Desktop/sql.log" ;
					r = MBS(
						"Text.AppendTextFile" ;
						"Error " &
						errorCode &
						": " &
						errorText &
						" while running " &
						sql &
						¶ &
						¶ ;
						path
					)
				] ;
				""
			)
		)
	] ;
	""
)

// to be used after a MBS() call with SQL to log the error.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Build a JSON after causing an error:
examples.text_code:
Let([
sql = "INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)";
r =
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteFileSQL
"; ""; sql; 9; 13; "Joe"; 23);
errorCode =
MBS
( "
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError
" );
errorMessage =
MBS
("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage");
lastSQL =
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL
");
params =
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters
");
json = JSONSetElement ( "{}" ;
["Parameters"; params; JSONArray];
["ErrorCode"; errorCode; JSONNumber];
["SQL"; lastSQL; JSONString];
["ErrorMessage"; errorMessage; JSONString])
]; JSONFormatElements ( json ))
Example result:
{
"ErrorCode"
:
8309
,
"ErrorMessage"
:
"ERROR: FQL0002/(1:12): The table named \"
Test\
" does not exist."
,
"Parameters"
: [
"Joe"
,
23
],
"SQL"
:
"INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)"
}
examples.xml_code:

<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  sql = "INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)";
  r = MBS("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; sql; 9; 13; "Joe"; 23);

  errorCode = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError" );
  errorMessage = MBS("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage");
  lastSQL = MBS("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL");
  params = MBS("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters");

  json = JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; 
    ["Parameters"; params; JSONArray]; 
    ["ErrorCode"; errorCode; JSONNumber]; 
    ["SQL"; lastSQL; JSONString]; 
    ["ErrorMessage"; errorMessage; JSONString])
]; JSONFormatElements ( json ))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters
functions.description: Queries parameters from last failed SQL command. This allows you to debug problems with MBS functions doing multi commands, so e.g. you can see which values the failed record for an insert. When an error happens, we serialize the parameters used for the SQL operation. Value is empty if no parameters have been recorded. See FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL for the corresponding SQL statement and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage for the error message.

examples.label: Try it:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("
FM.InsertRecord
"; ""; "NonExistingTable"; "test"; 123; "other"; "abc") & ¶ &
MBS
("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters")
Example result:
[MBS] ERROR: FQL0002/(1:12): The table named "NonExistingTable" does not exist.
[123, "abc"]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.InsertRecord"; ""; "NonExistingTable"; "test"; 123; "other"; "abc") & ¶ &
MBS("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Check the last parameters after causing an error:
examples.text_code:
Let([
sql = "INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)";
r =
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteFileSQL
"; ""; sql; 9; 13; "Joe"; 23);
params =
MBS
("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters")
]; params)
Example result:
[
"Joe"
,
23
]

  sql = "INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)";
  r = MBS("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; sql; 9; 13; "Joe"; 23);

    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Build a JSON after causing an error:
examples.text_code:
Let([
sql = "INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)";
r =
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteFileSQL
"; ""; sql; 9; 13; "Joe"; 23);
errorCode =
MBS
( "
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError
" );
errorMessage =
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage
");
lastSQL =
MBS
("
FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL
");
params =
MBS
("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters");
json = JSONSetElement ( "{}" ;
["Parameters"; params; JSONArray];
["ErrorCode"; errorCode; JSONNumber];
["SQL"; lastSQL; JSONString];
["ErrorMessage"; errorMessage; JSONString])
]; JSONFormatElements ( json ))
Example result:
{
"ErrorCode"
:
8309
,
"ErrorMessage"
:
"ERROR: FQL0002/(1:12): The table named \"
Test\
" does not exist."
,
"Parameters"
: [
"Joe"
,
23
],
"SQL"
:
"INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)"
}
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
  sql = "INSERT INTO Test (FirstName, Age) VALUES (?,?)";
  r = MBS("FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; ""; sql; 9; 13; "Joe"; 23);

  errorCode = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError" );
  errorMessage = MBS("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage");
  lastSQL = MBS("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL");
  params = MBS("FM.ExecuteSQL.LastParameters");

  json = JSONSetElement ( "{}" ; 
    ["Parameters"; params; JSONArray]; 
    ["ErrorCode"; errorCode; JSONNumber]; 
    ["SQL"; lastSQL; JSONString]; 
    ["ErrorMessage"; errorMessage; JSONString])
]; JSONFormatElements ( json ))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL
functions.description: Queries last SQL command. This is for executing SQL in FileMaker and provides you the last SQL command as run by the plugin.

examples.label: Custom function to log the last SQL error to a log file:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
	[
		e = MBS( "IsError" ) ;
		r = If(
			e ;
			Let(
				[
					errorCode = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError" ) ;
					errorText = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage" ) ;
					sql = MBS( "FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL" ) ;
					path = "/Users/cs/Desktop/sql.log" ;
					r = MBS(
						"Text.AppendTextFile" ;
						"Error " &
						errorCode &
						": " &
						errorText &
						" while running " &
						sql &
						¶ &
						¶ ;
						path
					)
				] ;
				""
			)
		)
	] ;
	""
)

// to be used after a MBS() call with SQL to log the error.</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.FieldStatistics
functions.description: Queries statistics on a field. Same as FM.TableStatistics , but only for one field you picked.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to check.

parameters.name: FieldName
parameters.description: The name of the field to find.

functions.name: FM.Get
functions.description: Queries a Get() function by ID. Please pass an ID to return the value of the matching Get() function.

parameters.name: ID
parameters.description: The ID for the Get functions.

functions.name: FM.GetDisableContextualMenus
functions.description: Queries whether to disable contextual menus. Returns 1 if MBS Plugin disables contextual menus.

functions.name: FM.InitMessageFilter
functions.description: Initializes COM message filter. If a COM message can't be handled right away, COM may show a standard dialog to retry, switch task or cancel, which may bring your app in an endless dialog series. MBS Plugin implements a message filter which delays messages till the current processing is done and avoids the dialog. So long running options can perform well. Call this once if you see "server busy" error dialogs from COM subsystem on Windows.

functions.name: FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord"; FileName; TableName; IDField; IDValue; FieldName...; FieldValue... )
functions.description: Inserts or updates a record in a table in one line. If the insert fails due to duplicate ID, we will update the record values. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must be unique. Then you pass one pair of field name and value for each field you like to set in the new record. This function combines FM.InsertRecord and FM.UpdateRecord . If the ID is already used, we update, else we insert a new record. For one ID field, please use FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord, for two ID fields FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord2 and for three ID fields FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord3 . This function is broken in version 6.4 as it returns always a SQL error for insert. Please use 6.5 or newer. With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat FieldName and FieldValue parameters as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: IDField
parameters.description: The name of the field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue
parameters.description: The value for the ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

parameters.name: FieldName...
parameters.description: A field name to set. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldValue...
parameters.description: A field value to use for setting the field in the parameter before. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

examples.label: Update a record:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord";
""; "Asset Management"; /* file and table name */
"Asset ID"; "1"; /* ID field and value */
"Information"; "Just a test"; /* first field and value */
"Category"; "BestItems" ) /* second field and value */
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord"; 
""; "Asset Management"; /* file and table name */
"Asset ID"; "1"; /* ID field and value */
"Information"; "Just a test"; /* first field and value */
"Category"; "BestItems" ) /* second field and value */</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Insert or update record with 1 key field and 4 fields:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord"; $fileName; $tableName; 
$field1; $value1; // <- primary key field name and value
$field2; $value2; $field3; $value3; $field4; $value4; $field5; $value5)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Set a field with red bold text:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord"; 
Get(FileName); "Contacts"; 
"ID"; Contacts::ID; 
"First"; TextStyleAdd ( TextColor (  "Hello" ; RGB ( 255 ; 0 ; 0 ) ); bold ) )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>
