functions.name: FM.InsertOrUpdateRecordQuery
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.InsertOrUpdateRecordQuery"; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; KeyFieldMapping; QueryFileName; SQL Statement { ; Params... } )
functions.description: Insert or updates a lot of records queried from a database. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from one table to another table. Copy can be from one database to other or within same database. Also from one table to other or within a table. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Filename parameters can be empty. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Please remove from SQL statement and field names all fields which are globals or unstored calculations. For inserting records from other database (e.g. MySQL), please use SQL.InsertRecords function. You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. Please don't overload the function, so better work in blocks of e.g. 1000 rows at a time. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the target table. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into (target). Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: KeyFieldMapping
parameters.description: The list of field names with index to map keys. Please specify a list of key name=key index. The key name is the name of the field in the insert table and the index gives zero-based position in result set from SELECT query in 6th parameter. Field order in source and destination does not need to be the same.

parameters.name: QueryFileName
parameters.description: The file name for the database to run the query against (source). Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string to query records from the source table.

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

examples.label: Copies records from Contacts to ContactsCopy:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.InsertOrUpdateRecordQuery"; ""; "ContactsCopy"; "ContactsCopyID¶First¶Last¶Mobile"; "ContactsCopyID=0"; ""; "SELECT \"ContactsID\", \"First\", \"Last\", \"Mobile\" FROM \"Contacts\""  ) ]

    </Value>

// source file

functions.name: FM.InsertRecord
functions.description: Inserts a new record in a table in one line. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). Then you pass one pair of field name and value for each field you like to set in the new record. As we can't know the new ID assigned for the record, you can help yourself by passing in an UUID for a field and later select that to get the auto assigned primary key (or have the UUID be the primary key, so you don't need a query). See also FM.UpdateRecord to update a record and FM.InsertOrUpdateRecord to update or insert record. With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. The SQL functions in FileMaker do not trigger OnWindowsTransaction script trigger. Does not take part in transactions in FileMaker. Changes are made directly to the database, so you can't revert them later with Revert Transaction script step. Please consider using newer JSON.InsertRecord and JSON.InsertRecords functions. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat FieldName and FieldValue parameters as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldName...
parameters.description: A field name to set. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldValue...
parameters.description: A field value to use for setting the field in the parameter before. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

      <Calculation>Evaluate($Command) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Result" </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$Result </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: CountScriptCall custom function:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If($$LogScriptCalls; 
Let ( [
FileName = Get(FileName); 
ScriptName = Get(ScriptName); 

// try to insert new record
r = MBS( "FM.InsertRecord"; FileName; "ScriptCallCounter"; "Counter"; 1; "ScriptName"; ScriptName; "FileName"; FileName );

// r shows OK or "[MBS] ERROR:  (504): Field failed unique value validation test" in case of duplicate

// check for error: 504
p = Position ( r ; "(504)" ; 1 ; 1 );

// update existing call on error
r = If( p > 0; MBS( "FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; FileName; "UPDATE \"ScriptCallCounter\" SET \"Counter\" = \"Counter\" + 1 WHERE \"ScriptName\" = ? AND \"FileName\" = ?"; 9; 13; ScriptName; FileName ); r) 

]; r ); "")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: LogFunctionCall custom function:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If($$LogScriptCalls; Let ( [

// query some values about current context
FileName = Get(FileName); 
ScriptName = Get(ScriptName); 
TableName = Get(LayoutTableName); 
AccountName = Get(AccountName); 
LayoutName = Get(LayoutName); 
UserName = Get(UserName); 
IP = Get(SystemIPAddress); 
ScriptParameter = Get(ScriptParameter); 

// make an unique key for later
$ScriptCallID = Get(UUID);

// and build call stack
$$CallStack = $$CallStack & ScriptName & ¶;

// now log it.
r = MBS( "FM.InsertRecord"; FileName; "ScriptCallLog"; 
"ScriptCallID"; $ScriptCallID; 
"ScriptName"; ScriptName; 
"FileName"; FileName; 
"TableName"; TableName; 
"LayoutName"; LayoutName; 
"AccountName"; AccountName; 
"UserName"; UserName; 
"IP"; IP; 
"ScriptParameter"; GetAsText(ScriptParameter);
"CallStack"; $$CallStack;
"StartTime"; Get ( CurrentTimestamp ))

]; r ); "")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: LogFunctionResult custom function:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If( not IsEmpty($ScriptCallID); Let ( [

// remove us from call stack
$$CallStack = LeftValues ( $$CallStack ; ValueCount ( $$CallStack ) - 1 );

// now updat entry in log to show result.
FileName = Get(FileName); 

r = MBS( "FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; FileName; "UPDATE \"ScriptCallLog\" SET \"ScriptResult\" = ?, \"EndTime\" = ? WHERE \"ScriptCallID\" = ?"; 9; 13; GetAsText( Result ); Get(CurrentTimestamp); $ScriptCallID )

]; Result ); Result)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.InsertRecordCSV
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.InsertRecordCSV"; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; Text { ; Delimiter; FieldName...; FieldValue... } )
functions.description: Inserts records based on CSV text. This function allows for easy importing of text to create a lot of records. Filename parameters can be empty. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Since version 8.2 we can fill text, number, time, timestamp and date fields and let FileMaker convert the values with your current locale. Returns number of records added. CSV is automatically parsed per line with List.CSVSplit function internally. You can pass one pair of field name and value for each extra field you like to set in the new records. e.g. a reference ID. You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. Version 8.5 adds Delimiter parameter, which may break scripts, but we need to have that parameter for people with older version. Version 11.1 does handle backslashes inside quotes for \r and \n for returns and \t for tabs. You can use \\ for backslash. This function uses our field type cache from the Audit functions to determinate the type of a field. If you change a field type, you may restart FileMaker or use Audit.ClearCaches to clear it. If input is stored in a container, you can use Text.ReadTextFromContainer function to read text first and pass it to this function. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat FieldName and FieldValue parameters as often as you need.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. If you like to skip a field, you may include a field name multiple time, so the first assignment will then be overwritten by the following assignment.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to import in TSV/CSV format. This means tab, comma or semicolon character between fields and newline character for new records. Use Files.ReadTextFile if needed to read this from a file on disk.

parameters.name: Delimiter
parameters.description: The delimiter to use. If empty, we auto detect it.

parameters.name: FieldName...
parameters.description: A field name to set.

parameters.name: FieldValue...
parameters.description: A field value to use for setting the field in the parameter before. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

functions.name: FM.InsertRecordQuery
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.InsertRecordQuery"; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; QueryFileName; SQL Statement { ; Params... } )
functions.description: Inserts a lot of records queried from a database. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from one table to another table. Copy can be from one database to other or within same database. Also from one table to other or within a table. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Filename parameters can be empty. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Please avoid field names of global or unstored calculated fields in the FieldNames list. For inserting records from other database (e.g. MySQL), please use SQL.InsertRecords function. Using FM.SetSQLBatchMode you can let the plugin insert e.g. 50 rows in one chunk which can speed up insert a lot. Due to memory usage it can be useful to segment copy queries to only copy one GB at a time and avoid heavy memory pressure. With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. Please don't overload the function, so better work in blocks of e.g. 1000 rows at a time. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: QueryFileName
parameters.description: The file name for the database to run the query against. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

functions.name: FM.InsertRecordQueryIgnoreDuplicates
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.InsertRecordQueryIgnoreDuplicates"; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; QueryFileName; SQL Statement { ; Params... } )
functions.description: Inserts a lot of records queried from a database. Same as FM.InsertRecordQuery , but ignores duplicate records. On success returns the number of duplicate records instead of OK if there were any. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from one table to another table. Copy can be from one database to other or within same database. Also from one table to other or within a table. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Filename parameters can be empty. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Does not copy text styles, only plain text for text fields. The duplicate check is based on FileMaker checking if a field is unique, e.g. the record ID. For inserting records from other database (e.g. MySQL), please use SQL.InsertRecords function. With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. Please don't overload the function, so better work in blocks of e.g. 1000 rows at a time. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: QueryFileName
parameters.description: The file name for the database to run the query against. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

    </Repetition>

  </Step>

functions.name: FM.InsertRecordTSV
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.InsertRecordTSV"; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; Text { ; FieldName...; FieldValue... } )
functions.description: Inserts records based on tab/return separated text. This function allows for easy importing of text to create a lot of records. Filename parameters can be empty. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Since version 8.2 we can fill text, number, time, timestamp and date fields and let FileMaker convert the values with your current locale. Returns number of records added. For XML, please check XML.Import function. For splitting values with delimiter into a list, please use QuickList.New which also works with comma, tab, newline or other characters. With plugin version 6.2 or newer you can pass one pair of field name and value for each extra field you like to set in the new records. e.g. a reference ID. With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. Version 11.1 does handle backslashes inside quotes for \r and \n for returns and \t for tabs. You can use \\ for backslash. This function uses our field type cache from the Audit functions to determinate the type of a field. If you change a field type, you may restart FileMaker or use Audit.ClearCaches to clear it. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat FieldName and FieldValue parameters as often as you need.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to import in TSV format. This means tab character between fields and newline character for new records

parameters.name: FieldName...
parameters.description: Additional field to set with the import. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldValue...
parameters.description: A field value to use for setting the field in the parameter before. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

examples.label: Import records from text:

Delete All Records [No dialog]
Set Variable [
$fields
; Value:"FirstName¶LastName¶City"]
Set Variable [
$r

$fields
;
Import::ImportText
)]

functions.name: FM.InsertSetUpdateProgressDialog
functions.description: Enable updating progress bar. If you enable progress dialog updates, the functions FM.InsertRecordQuery , FM.InsertRecordQueryIgnoreDuplicates and FM.InsertRecordTSV will update progress dialog. If cancel button is clicked, the functions may cancel and return an error. Depending on event handling a cancel button may not work while a plugin function runs! You can use Base/Factor to adjust updates in progress dialog. e.g. if you have 4 things to do and FM.InsertRecordTSV is 2nd one, you can use a base of 25 (so 25% is for first thing to do) and factor of 0.25. Our progress will go from 25 to 50% than.

parameters.name: Update
parameters.description: Pass 1 to update Progress Dialog. Pass 0 to disable feature.

parameters.name: Base
parameters.description: The progress base in percent. Default is 0.

parameters.name: Factor
parameters.description: The factor to apply for progress update. Default is 1.

functions.name: FM.Loop
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.Loop"; Variable Name; Start Value; End Value; Step Value; Expression { ; ExitIfExpression } )
functions.description: Universal function to run a loop with evaluate. Counts number up/down from start to end value. Evaluates each time the formula with new variable value. If trace is enabled and plugin writes log entries for each evaluate, things are slower than without tracing. For best performance, do not use trace with this function. e.g. call Trace.Off function. Returns empty text if End value is zero and step is positive to allow for 0 time loops. Similar to the While() function in FileMaker 18.

parameters.name: Variable Name
parameters.description: The name of the variable to use. Can be with zero, one or zwei $ on the start.

parameters.name: Start Value
parameters.description: The start value of the loop.

parameters.name: End Value
parameters.description: The end value of the loop.

parameters.name: Step Value
parameters.description: The number to add on each loop. Must not be zero. Can be positive to count forward or negative to count backwards.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

parameters.name: ExitIfExpression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate to check for exit early. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.result: Returns list of results or error.

examples.label: Loop to place month names in a PDF table:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.Loop"; 
/* variable */ "index"; 
/* start */ 1; 
/* end */ 12; 
/* step */ 1; 
/* formula */
"MBS(\"DynaPDF.Table.SetCellText\"; $table; $rowNum; index; \"center\"; \"center\"; GetValue($months; index))")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Loop with Let for multiple commands
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.Loop"; 
/* variable */ "index"; 
/* start */ 1; 
/* end */ 31; 
/* step */ 1; 
/* formula */
"Let([$rowNum = MBS(\"DynaPDF.Table.AddRow\"; $table); 
r = MBS(\"DynaPDF.Table.SetCellText\"; $table; $rowNum; 0; \"center\"; \"center\"; index)]; r)")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

"index/10")</Calculation>

examples.label: Inner and outer loop:
examples.text_code:
Let( [
$Expression1
= "
MBS
(\"FM.Loop\"; \"j\"; 1; 3; 1;
$Expression2
)";
$Expression2
= "
$i
& \" * \" & j & \" = \" & (
$i
* j)"
//
$i
in outer loop must be with $ so it's valid in the inner loop
];
MBS
("FM.Loop"; "
$i
"; 1; 4; 1;
$Expression1
))
Example result:
1 * 1 = 1
1 * 2 = 2
1 * 3 = 3
2 * 1 = 2
2 * 2 = 4
2 * 3 = 6
3 * 1 = 3
3 * 2 = 6
3 * 3 = 9
4 * 1 = 4
4 * 2 = 8
4 * 3 = 12
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let( [ 

$Expression1 = "MBS(\"FM.Loop\"; \"j\"; 1; 3; 1; $Expression2)";
$Expression2 = " $i & \" * \" & j & \" = \" & ($i * j)"
// $i in outer loop must be with $ so it's valid in the inner loop
];

MBS("FM.Loop"; "$i"; 1; 4; 1; $Expression1))</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: With an exit early expression:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.Loop"; 
/* variable */ "index"; 
/* start */ 1; 
/* end */ 12; 
/* step */ 1; 
/* formula */
"index*2";  // calculate this
"index >= 10") // exit if index gets big</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.MouseWheelScriptTrigger
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.MouseWheelScriptTrigger"; FileName; ScriptName { ; MinDelay; IgnoreRectangleX; IgnoreRectangleY; IgnoreRectangleW; IgnoreRectangleH } )
functions.description: Installs a script trigger for mouse wheel. If you like to have a script triggered to switch records on Windows with mouse wheel, you can use this function. The script parameter is the delta on mouse wheel usage. 120 is one tick down and negative value means movement up. The script is called at most once every second (by default). Turn off by calling again with empty script name. This setting is global, so you may just install once in start script. Add option to ignore a rectangle for plugin version 7.1. Implemented for Mac and FileMaker 16 or newer with MBS Plugin 9.1. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to trigger. Can be empty to disable feature.

parameters.name: MinDelay
parameters.description: The minimum delay between script triggers. Default is 1. Could be 0.5 for a quicker reaction.

parameters.name: IgnoreRectangleX
parameters.description: The X position of the rectangle where scroll action is ignored if the mouse is inside. Relative to the parent window. Default is -1 to have no such rectangle. Rectangle area is Windows only.

parameters.name: IgnoreRectangleY
parameters.description: The Y position of the rectangle where scroll action is ignored if the mouse is inside. Relative to the parent window. Default is -1 to have no such rectangle.

parameters.name: IgnoreRectangleW
parameters.description: The width of the rectangle where scroll action is ignored if the mouse is inside. Relative to the parent window. Default is -1 to have no such rectangle.

parameters.name: IgnoreRectangleH
parameters.description: The height of the rectangle where scroll action is ignored if the mouse is inside. Relative to the parent window. Default is -1 to have no such rectangle.

examples.label: Installs trigger:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("FM.MouseWheelScriptTrigger"; Get(FileName); "MouseTrigger")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("FM.MouseWheelScriptTrigger"; Get(FileName); "MouseTrigger")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.PatchNotificationProblem
functions.description: Patched problem with Scroll Notifications. FileMaker 16.0.4 on MacOS 10.13.3 has a bug that can cause crashes with scroll gestures. Crash reports show message about "terminating with uncaught exception" and stack trace shows _beginScrollGesture method called which posts a notification to a non-existing observer. The plugin, if activated with this function will watch for those notifications and just ignore them, so the observer is not called and the crash does not happen.

functions.name: FM.QueryBaseTableNames
functions.description: Queries the base table names of all files currently open. May be limited to what you are allowed to access. See also FM.QueryTableNames . Needs permissions to query your database schema via SQL.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The database file name to look into. If empty or missing, we look in current database. (new in 6.2)

functions.result: Returns names of tables separated by return.

examples.label: Query fields as JSON:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>While([
// query field list
~fields = MBS("FM.QueryFieldsForTableName"; Get(LayoutTableName));
$$json = "{}"
];
// loop over field list
ValueCount(~fields ) > 1;
[
// get field name without spaces and the value from the field
fieldName = Substitute(GetValue(~fields ; 1); " "; "");
fieldValue = Evaluate(Get(LayoutTableName) & "::" & GetValue(~fields; 1));
// add to json as text value
$$json = JSONSetElement ( $$json ; fieldName; fieldValue ; JSONString );
// next
~fields = MiddleValues ( ~fields ; 2; ValueCount(~fields )-1 )
];
$$json)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.RecordStatistics
functions.description: Queries statistics on a record. Same as FM.TableStatistics , but only for one record you selected. The plugin will walk count fields with various data types and how many empty fields are there. Skips global and formula fields. We sum up the length of all text in text fields as well as the text content of all fields. We sum up the size of all internal containers, so ContainerByteSize + AllTextLengths*2 plus some metadata is the size of the database file. For internal containers we also count the streams inside, so you know how many JPEGs and other file types are in the table. External containers in this function are these references by a file path. Containers stored external, but managed by FileMaker are considered internal ones from the point of view of the plugin. e.g. {

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to check.

parameters.name: PrimaryKeyName
parameters.description: The name of the primary key to find the record.

parameters.name: PrimaryKeyValue
parameters.description: The value of the primary key.

    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.RunDatabaseDesignReport
functions.description: Performs a data design report automatically. This must be the last step in a script, because it triggers an asynchronously process to make a report. So the plugin will run the menu command, set the options, press the create button and the save button for you. If you like to run other things later, simply run a script 5 seconds later with FM.RunScriptLater . FileMaker and the dialogs may need to be frontmost to have this work well. You may want to call Process.SetFrontMost to make sure FileMaker is front. May not work if debugger window is visible. See also FM.RunSaveAsXML for FileMaker 18 or newer. Needs accessibility permissions in system preferences to click save button for MacOS Catalina and may not set path there. Fixed directory path passing for macOS in plugin version 10.4. If path contains a file name, we pass that to the save dialog and if possible don't show the dialog at all. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: UseXML
parameters.description: Pass 1 to use XML or 0 to use HTML for the report.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The path where to save for MacOS. If you don't specify a path, the last one used in the dialogs will be used. No longer used on MacOS Mojave/Catalina due to raised security by Apple which prevents us changing the dialog.

parameters.name: OpenWhenDone
parameters.description: Whether to open file when done. Pass 1 to open file after creating DDR or 0 to not open. Default is 0.

parameters.name: FileNames
parameters.description: The list of filenames to set checkmarks for. Names must match exactly to be checked. MacOS only.

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.RunScript
functions.description: Runs a script in any open FileMaker file. You can also pass a text string to the Script as a Parameter. The parameter can be retrieved by using Get(ScriptParameter) in the script that plug-in is calling. The basic concept behind triggering scripts with a plug-in is as follows. In order for the Plug-in to call a script it must be part of a calculation that is evaluated. For example to cause a script to run when a field is exited, you place the MBS function call in the auto-enter calculation of the field you want to serve as the trigger. When the field is exited the Auto-enter calculation is evaluated and the MBS function is fired off.. Placing the function call in the Field Validation calculation of the trigger field will cause the script to run on Record Exit. NOT on field Exit. This is because Validations by calculation happen at when the record is exited. Anywhere you have access to the calculation dialog you can place a Script trigger function. When the calculation is evaluated the script will run. This includes the new conditional formatting calculations that can be placed on many layout objects. While this opens up new opportunities, you might find that your script is being called continuously. see also FM.RunScriptIdle , FM.RunScriptLater and Schedule functions. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run. Or the ID of the script in the current file.

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: The Parameter to Pass to the script.

parameters.name: ScriptControl
parameters.description: The script control mode. Can be Pause, Halt, Exit or Resume. Default is Pause. May be ignored by FileMaker.

examples.label: Trigger a script
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
trigger = MBS("FM.RunScript"; Get(FileName); "Triggered Script"; "")
];
1
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Trigger script by ID:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.RunScript"; "test.fmp12"; "12" /* PrintRecords */; "Hello World")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.RunScript"; "test.fmp12"; "12" /* PrintRecords */; "Hello World")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Registers a hotkey option-control-command-c to run a script if the right file name is current:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Hotkey
; Value:
MBS
("
HotKey.Register
"; "C"; "option control command") ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
HotKey.SetEvaluate
";
$Hotkey
; "Let([ name = Get(FileName); r = if(name = \"Hotkeys\";
MBS
( \"FM.RunScript\"; \"HotKeys\"; \"test\"; \"Hello World\"); \"Wrong file\") ]; r)") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("Hotkey.Register"; "C"; "option control command") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Hotkey</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("HotKey.SetEvaluate"; $Hotkey; "Let([ name = Get(FileName); r = if(name = \"Hotkeys\"; MBS( \"FM.RunScript\"; \"HotKeys\"; \"test\"; \"Hello World\"); \"Wrong file\") ]; r)") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.RunScriptIdle
functions.description: Runs a script after idle period. This script is called in FileMaker whenever no user activity has been for the given amount of time. You can also pass a text string to the Script as a Parameter. The parameter can be retrieved by using Get(ScriptParameter) in the script that plug-in is calling. see also Schedule.StartScriptOnIdle , FM.StopScriptIdle , FM.RunScript , FM.RunScriptLater and Schedule functions. Once you have the script running, you can use Process.IsFrontMost function to check if FileMaker is in the foreground or not. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: DelaySeconds
parameters.description: The Number of Seconds to wait before running the script. Moving the mouse or using keyboard will reset the counter.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run. Or the ID of the script in the current file.

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: The Script Parameter to pass to the script

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error message on failure.

examples.label: Start idle script:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.RunScriptIdle"; 10; Get(FileName); "Trigger")]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.RunScriptIdle"; 10; Get(FileName); "Trigger")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.RunScriptLater
functions.description: Runs a script in any open FileMaker file. You can also pass a text string to the Script as a Parameter. The parameter can be retrieved by using Get(ScriptParameter) in the script that plug-in is calling. see also Schedule.StartScriptAfterDelay , FM.RunScriptIdle , FM.RunScript and Schedule functions. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: DelaySeconds
parameters.description: The Number of Seconds to wait before running the script.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the file that contains the script to be run

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The exact name of the script to run. Or the ID of the script in the current file.

parameters.name: ScriptParameter
parameters.description: Optional, he Script Parameter to pass to the script

functions.result: Returns OK on success or error message on failure.

examples.label: Trigger a script later

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
/*-----------------PARAMETERS--------------------*/
DelaySeconds = 5;
FileName = Get ( FileName );
ScriptName = "Triggered Script Demo 2";
ScriptParameter = "Some Data"
];
/*-------------------FUNCTION----------------------*/
MBS( 
"FM.RunScriptLater";
DelaySeconds;
FileName;
ScriptName;
ScriptParameter
)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.Avg
functions.description: Calculates average value in a record set. You got a record set in memory and we can walk over a column in a given row range to add the sum value and later divide by number of values.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

examples.label: Query once and make several calculations:
examples.text_code:
# let FileMaker query data once
Set Variable [
$Records
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT Price FROM Produkte") ]
# Now get sum, min, max and avg
Set Variable [
$sum
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Sum
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$min
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Min
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$max
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Max
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$avg
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.Avg";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Text
";
$records
; "" /* firstRow */; "" /* lastRow */; "" /* firstCol */; "" /* lastCol */; ", " /* rowSep */) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$records
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Sum: " &
$sum
& ¶ & "Min: " &
$min
& ¶ & "Max: " &
$max
& ¶ & "Avg: " &
$avg
& ¶ & "text: " &
$text
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> let FileMaker query data once</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; Get(FileName); "SELECT Price FROM Produkte") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Records</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Now get sum, min, max and avg</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Sum"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sum</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Min"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$min</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Max"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$max</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Avg"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$avg</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Text"; $records; "" /* firstRow */; "" /* lastRow */; "" /* firstCol */; "" /* lastCol */; ", " /* rowSep */) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $records ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Sum: " & $sum & ¶ & "Min: " & $min & ¶ & "Max: " & $max & ¶ & "Avg: " & $avg & ¶ & "text: " & $text </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.CSV
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.CSV"; SQLref { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol; rowSeparator; colSeparator; Flags } )
functions.description: Queries text of SQL result for CSV export. You can use this method to quickly get all fields in a given area as text. If needed, we put values in quotes and escape quotes and newlines. For numeric values we ask FileMaker to provide text representation, so FileMaker may use dot or comma as decimal divider depending on your locale. Please use Text.WriteTextFile to write to text file if you need CSV export.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1 if no value is given.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is FM.SQL.FieldCount -1 if no value is given.

parameters.name: rowSeparator
parameters.description: The row separator. Default is CR. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: colSeparator
parameters.description: The column separator. Default is semicolon. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags. 1: Pass 1 to put all text values in quotes.

examples.label: Query fields and export as text and CSV:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "select * from Clients")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["SQL error";
$sql
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["SQL result"; "got " &
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.RowCount
";
$sql
) &" rows with each " &
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.FieldCount
";
$sql
) & " …"]
# Write to text file
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Text
";
$sql
)]
Set Variable [
$desktopFolder
; Value:
MBS
( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" )]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$desktopFolder
; "fields.txt" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.WriteTextFile
";
$text
;
$path
; "UTF8")]
# Write to text file
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.CSV";
$sql
)]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$desktopFolder
; "fields.csv" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.WriteTextFile
";
$text
;
$path
; "UTF8")]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql
)]
End If
examples.xml_code:

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "select * from Clients")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql</Name>

  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">

      <Calculation>"SQL error"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$sql</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"SQL result"; "got " & MBS( "FM.SQL.RowCount"; $sql ) &" rows with each " & MBS( "FM.SQL.FieldCount"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$sql ) & " …"</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write to text file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Text"; $sql )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Folders.UserDesktop" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$desktopFolder</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $desktopFolder; "fields.txt" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; $text; $path; "UTF8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Write to text file</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.CSV"; $sql )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.AddPathComponent"; $desktopFolder; "fields.csv" )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.WriteTextFile"; $text; $path; "UTF8")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Cleanup</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $sql )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.Execute
functions.description: Execute the SQL Statement against a FileMaker database. Returns a SQL Reference, so you can use functions like FM.SQL.RowCount , FM.SQL.FieldCount , FM.SQL.Field and FM.SQL.FieldType to query result. Using FM.SQL.Execute avoids converting all result values to text like in FM.ExecuteSQL or FM.ExecuteFileSQL functions. Here you get result set stored in memory and back a reference number, so you can query individual values later. Like getting back container value and using it without just getting the file name as text. Don't forget to use FM.SQL.Release to release the result from memory when you are done with it. This function requires FileMaker 11 or newer. Please use FM.ExecuteSQL on FileMaker 10 or older. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Params parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: SQL Statement
parameters.description: SQL Statement as a Text string

parameters.name: Params...
parameters.description: Optional, pass here parameters. One parameter to this function for each parameter you want to pass to the SQL statement.

examples.label: Query record as JSON:
examples.text_code:
Let ( [
fields =
MBS
( "
FM.QueryFieldsForTableName
"; "Contacts");
Records =
MBS
( "FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "SELECT \"" & substitute(fields; ¶; "\", \"") & "\" FROM \"Contacts\" WHERE \"First\" = ?"; "Joe");
json =
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.JSONRecord
"; Records; 0; fields);
r =
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
"; Records )]
; json )
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let ( [
fields = MBS( "FM.QueryFieldsForTableName"; "Contacts");
Records = MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "SELECT \"" & substitute(fields; ¶; "\", \"") & "\" FROM \"Contacts\" WHERE \"First\" = ?"; "Joe");

json = MBS( "FM.SQL.JSONRecord"; Records; 0; fields);
r = MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; Records )]
; json )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Make a JSON query in one Let statment:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let (
	[
		$sdat = "10/1/2019 00:00";
		$edat = "10/10/2019 00:00";
		sku = "'PARbh50','PARgy50'";
		sql1 = MBS("FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "Select docnum,linetotal,db from \"MyTable\" where ItemCode in (" & sku & ") and DocDate between ? and ?"; $sdat; $edat);
		json = MBS( "FM.SQL.JSONRecords"; sql1; "DocNum¶LineTotal¶DB"; 1);
		r = MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; sql1 )
	] ; json
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Query related records as JSON:
examples.text_code:
# get related teams
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.Execute"; Get(FileName); "SELECT \"UUID\", \"ID\", \"ID_Department\", \"Team.Name\", \"MemberCount\" FROM \"Teams\" WHERE ID_Department=?";
Department::ID
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "SQL error" ;
$r
]
Else
# fill as json in field
Set Field [
Department::JSON_Department
;
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.JSONRecords
";
$r
; "UUID¶ID¶ID_Department¶Team.Name¶MemberCount") ]
Set Variable [
$e
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$r
) ]
End If

("IsError") ]

$SQL
; 1) ]

";

) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Values" ;

& ¶ &
$Groups
]
End If

examples.label: Query current record as JSON:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let([
// get field list
fields = MBS( "FM.QueryFieldsForTableName"; Get(LayoutTableName));
// query the fields using that fieold list for current record
sql = MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; Get(FileName); "SELECT \"" & Substitute(fields; ¶; "\", \"") & "\" FROM \"" & Get ( LayoutTableName ) & "\" WHERE RowID = ?"; Get( RecordID ));
// and query JSON
json = MBS( "FM.SQL.JSONRecord"; sql; 0; fields );
// free SQL result
R = MBS("FM.SQL.Release"; sql)
]; json)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.Field
functions.description: Queries field value for given position in result. Native data type is preserved so this function can returns text, number, time, date, timestamp and container.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row number from 0 to FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The column number from 0 to FM.SQL.FieldCount -1.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: Various flags. Pass 0 to return value as it is. (default) Pass 1 to return file name of a container field. Pass 2 to return container data base64 encoded.

examples.label: Query field values:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute

functions.name: FM.SQL.InsertOrUpdateRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.InsertOrUpdateRecords"; SQLref; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames; KeyFieldMapping )
functions.description: Insert or updates a lot of records queried from a database. Similar to FM.InsertOrUpdateRecordQuery , but taking records from a stored SQL result. Copy can be from one database to other or within same database. Also from one table to other or within a table. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Filename parameters can be empty. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the stored SQL result. Please remove from SQL statement and field names all fields which are globals or unstored calculations. For inserting records from other database (e.g. MySQL), please use SQL.InsertRecords function. You can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. You can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: KeyFieldMapping
parameters.description: The list of field names with index to map keys. Please specify a list of key name=key index. The key name is the name of the field in the insert table and the index gives zero-based position in result set from SELECT query in 6th parameter. Field order in source and destination does not need to be the same.

examples.label: Try the insert and update:
examples.text_code:
# Load records into a stored SQL result and insert/update into other table
Set Variable [
$SelectSQL
; Value: "SELECT PrimaryKey, TextField, NumberField, DateField, TimeField, TimestampField, ContainerField, Description FROM Source" ]
Set Variable [
$InsertFields
; Value: "Key¶TextField¶NumberField¶DateField¶TimeField¶TimestampField¶ContainerField¶Description" ]
#
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
("
FM.SQL.Execute
"; Get(FileName);
$SelectSQL
) ]
#
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$sql
]
Else
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("FM.SQL.InsertOrUpdateRecords";
$sql
; Get(FileName); "Dest";
$InsertFields
; "Key=0") ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$result
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Records updated." ;
$result
]
End If
End If
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
("
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql
) ]

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldNames

parameters.name: KeyFieldMapping
parameters.description: The list of field names with index to map keys. Please specify a list of key name=key index. The key name is the name of the field in the insert table and the index gives zero-based position in result set from SELECT query in 6th parameter. Field order in source and destination does not need to be the same.

functions.name: FM.SQL.InsertRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.InsertRecords"; SQLref; InsertFileName; InsertTableName; FieldNames { ; StartRow; EndRow } )
functions.description: Inserts records in FileMaker database from records in memory. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from a query in FileMaker database into a FileMaker table. Please provide field names in FileMaker. The order has to match those in the record set. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastErrorMessage and FM.ExecuteSQL.LastError to see error message. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Function will fail if data types in source and dest fields do not match for assignment. e.g. date and time fields will not work, only timestamp fields. For insert from SQL Connection into FileMaker database, please use SQL.InsertRecords . Please don't overload the function, so better work in blocks of e.g. 1000 rows at a time.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: InsertFileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the insert table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored.

parameters.name: StartRow
parameters.description: The index of the start row in the current recordset. First row is 0.

parameters.name: EndRow
parameters.description: The index of the end row in the current recordset. First row is 0. Last Row would be FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

examples.label: Copy records:
examples.text_code:
# select some data
Set Variable [
$Records
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "SELECT FirstName, LastName, Birthday FROM test" )]
# now insert them somewhere else
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.InsertRecords";
$Records
; ""; "test"; "FirstName¶LastName¶BirthDay")]
# and free record set
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$Records
)]

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Records</Name>

    <Text> now insert them somewhere else</Text>

      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.InsertRecords"; $Records; ""; "test"; "FirstName¶LastName¶BirthDay")</Calculation>

    </Repetition>

functions.name: FM.SQL.InsertRecordsToSQL
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.InsertRecordsToSQL"; SQLref; Connection; InsertTableName; FieldNames { ; StartRow; EndRow; Replace } )
functions.description: Inserts records in SQL database from records in memory. This function allows to easily copy a lot of records from a query in FileMaker database into a SQL database. Please provide field names in other table. The order has to match those in the record set. Due to passing in new field names, you can even rearrange values from one column to other while copying. In the SQL you can use functions for sums or join data from several tables together to fill a new table. Reports an error if field name list doesn't match column count of the query result. Function will fail if data types in source and dest fields do not match for assignment. e.g. date and time fields will not work, only timestamp fields. For insert within FileMaker, please use FM.SQL.InsertRecords . Please don't overload the function, so better work in blocks of e.g. 1000 rows at a time.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: Connection
parameters.description: The connection reference number gained with SQL.NewConnection .

parameters.name: InsertTableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the insert. Empty entries in the list are ignored.

parameters.name: StartRow
parameters.description: The index of the start row. First row is 0. Pass -1 or nothing for default which is starting with row 0.

parameters.name: EndRow
parameters.description: The index of the end row. First row is 0. Last Row would be FM.SQL.RowCount -1. Pass -1 to use all rows and the plugin will internally use RowCount-1.

parameters.name: Replace
parameters.description: Pass for regular INSERT statements. Set to 1 to get REPLACE statements, which overwrite existing entries. Currently only available for MySQL and databases with same syntax.

examples.label: Insert Records to SQL database:
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.InsertRecordsToSQL"; $Records; $connection; $BaseTableName; $FieldNames)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$InsertResult</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Copy records to SQLite:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$Connection
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.NewConnection
") ]
# Tell plugin where SQLite library is
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.InternalSQLiteLibrary.Activate
") ]
# Tell plugin we want to use SQLite
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.SetClient
";
$Connection
; "SQLite") ]
# Connect to database in read/write/create mode. Creates new file if none exists.
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Connect
";
$Connection
; "/Users/cs/Desktop/mydatabase.sqlite") ]
If [
$result
= "OK" ]
# Query records in FileMaker
Set Variable [
$Records
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT FirstName, LastName, Birthday FROM People") ]
# Transfer to other database
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.InsertRecordsToSQL";
$Records
;
$Connection
; "People"; "FirstName¶LastName¶Birthday") ]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.Commit
";
$Connection
) ]
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$Records
) ]
If [
$result
≠ "OK" ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Error: " &
$result
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog [ "Record exported." ]
End If
End If
Set Variable [
$result2
; Value:
MBS
("
SQL.FreeConnection
";
$Connection
) ]

  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.JSONRecord
functions.description: Returns a row as JSON object. You provide list of field names, which should be in same order as fields in SQL result. Optionally we can return SQL dates, times and timestamps as SQL format. Containers are returned as Base64 encoded data. See FM.SQL.XMLRecord for the same as XML.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row you like to get. 0 for first row.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the JSON. If you have duplicate entries in the field names, the resulting JSON will have duplicate keys, which can be a problem.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the json creation. Add 1 to get all values as text (empty text instead of null). Add 2 to get all dates, times and timestamps in SQL format (formatted by plugin) instead of GetAsText() by FileMaker. Add 4 to get arrays instead of objects for the individual rows. Add 8 to get containers as objects with name, size and data entries. (new in v12.1) Add 32 to detect JSON in text values and return the JSON inline. (New in v15.4)

examples.label: Query JSON record:
examples.text_code:
# run query to get some data
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "SELECT \"First\", \"Last\", Company, \"Photo Container\" FROM Contacts") ]
# query data
Set Variable [
$json
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.JSONRecord";
$sql
; 0; "First¶Last¶Company¶Photo"; 2) ]
# store result
Set Field [
Contacts::Output
;
$json
]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql
) ]
examples.xml_code:

  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "SELECT \"First\", \"Last\", Company, \"Photo Container\" FROM Contacts") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> query data</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.JSONRecord"; $sql; 0; "First¶Last¶Company¶Photo"; 2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> store result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$json </Calculation>
    <Field table="Contacts" id="99999" name="Output "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> free memory</Text>
  </Step>

    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $sql) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>

  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.JSONRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.JSONRecords"; SQLref; FieldNames { ; Flags; FirstRow; LastRow } )
functions.description: Returns rows as JSON array. You provide list of field names, which should be in same order as fields in SQL result. Optionally we can return SQL dates, times and timestamps as SQL format. Containers are returned as Base64 encoded data. See FM.SQL.XMLRecords for the same as XML.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the JSON. If you have duplicate entries in the field names, the resulting JSON will have duplicate keys, which can be a problem.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the json creation. Add 1 to get all values as text (empty text instead of null). Add 2 to get all dates, times and timestamps in SQL format (formatted by plugin) instead of GetAsText() by FileMaker. Add 4 to get arrays instead of objects for the individual rows. Add 8 to get containers as objects with name, size and data entries. (new in v12.1) Add 32 to detect JSON in text values and return the JSON inline. (New in v15.4)

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1.
