examples.label: Builds Assets as JSON with Assignments JSON array added:
examples.text_code:
# let us get Assets via SQL as JSON
Set Variable [
$sql1
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT * FROM Assets" ) ]
Set Variable [
$names1
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.ExecuteFileSQL
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT FieldName FROM FileMaker_Fields WHERE TableName = 'Assets' "; 9; 13) ]
Set Variable [
$json1
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.JSONRecords";
$sql1
;
$names1
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql1
) ]
#
# let us get Assignments via SQL as JSON
Set Variable [
$sql2
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT * FROM Assignments" ) ]
Set Variable [
$names2
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.ExecuteFileSQL
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT FieldName FROM FileMaker_Fields WHERE TableName = 'Assignments' "; 9; 13) ]
Set Variable [
$json2
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.JSONRecords";
$sql2
;
$names2
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql2
) ]
#
# how many Assets did we got?
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.GetArraySize
";
$json2
) ]
#
# not go and add an Assignments Array to each Assets entry
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$index
≤
$count
]
Loop [ Flush: Defer ]
# your script steps here
Set Variable [
$json1
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.SetPathItem
";
$json1
;
$Index
& ¶ & "Assignments"; "[]") ]
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
End If
#
# now loop over all assignments and insert them into assets
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$index
≤
$count
]
Loop [ Flush: Defer ]
# your script steps here
Set Variable [
$json3
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.GetArrayItem
";
$json2
;
$index
) ]
Set Field [
JSON::JSON3
;
$json3
]
# find the key to match
Set Variable [
$key
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.GetObjectItem
";
$json3
; "AssetForeignKey") ]
# and lookup the index in the json array for this primary key
Set Variable [
$DestIndex
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.FindValueInObjectArray
";
$json1
; "PrimaryKey";
$key
) ]
If [
MBS
("IsError") = 0 and
$DestIndex
≥ 0 ]
# add json to the array
Set Variable [
$json1
; Value:
MBS
( "
JSON.SetPathItem
";
$json1
;
$DestIndex
& ¶ & "Assignments¶append";
$json3
) ]
End If
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
End If
#
$json1
now has Assets records with Assignments as sub records in the JSON.
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> let us get Assets via SQL as JSON</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; Get(FileName); "SELECT * FROM Assets" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; Get(FileName); "SELECT FieldName FROM FileMaker_Fields WHERE TableName = 'Assets' "; 9; 13) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$names1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.JSONRecords"; $sql1; $names1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $sql1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> let us get Assignments via SQL as JSON</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; Get(FileName); "SELECT * FROM Assignments" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.ExecuteFileSQL"; Get(FileName); "SELECT FieldName FROM FileMaker_Fields WHERE TableName = 'Assignments' "; 9; 13) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$names2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.JSONRecords"; $sql2; $names2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $sql2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> how many Assets did we got?</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.GetArraySize"; $json2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> not go and add an Assignments Array to each Assets entry</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index ≤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Defer"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> your script steps here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.SetPathItem"; $json1; $Index & ¶ & "Assignments"; "[]") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> now loop over all assignments and insert them into assets</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index ≤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Defer"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> your script steps here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.GetArrayItem"; $json2; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json3</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$json3 </Calculation>
    <Field table="JSON" id="99999" name="JSON3 "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> find the key to match</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.GetObjectItem"; $json3; "AssetForeignKey") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> and lookup the index in the json array for this primary key</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.FindValueInObjectArray"; $json1; "PrimaryKey"; $key) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$DestIndex</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> MBS("IsError") = 0 and $DestIndex ≥ 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> add json to the array</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "JSON.SetPathItem"; $json1; $DestIndex & ¶ & "Assignments¶append"; $json3) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$json1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> $json1 now has Assets records with Assignments as sub records in the JSON.</Text>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.List
functions.description: Lists all IDs of current FM.SQL objects. Returns IDs for all FM.SQL objects in memory. If this list is growing, you may have forgotten to call FM.SQL.Release in your scripts. Normally after all scripts are done, this list should be empty.

examples.label: List all object IDs:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$list
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.List" ) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.List" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$list</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.Max
functions.description: Queries max value in a record set. You got a record set in memory and we can walk over a column in a given row range to find the maximum value.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

examples.label: Query once and make several calculations:
examples.text_code:
# let FileMaker query data once
Set Variable [
$Records
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT Price FROM Produkte") ]
# Now get sum, min, max and avg
Set Variable [
$sum
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Sum
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$min
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Min
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$max
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.Max";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$avg
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Avg
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Text
";
$records
; "" /* firstRow */; "" /* lastRow */; "" /* firstCol */; "" /* lastCol */; ", " /* rowSep */) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$records
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Sum: " &
$sum
& ¶ & "Min: " &
$min
& ¶ & "Max: " &
$max
& ¶ & "Avg: " &
$avg
& ¶ & "text: " &
$text
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> let FileMaker query data once</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; Get(FileName); "SELECT Price FROM Produkte") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Records</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Now get sum, min, max and avg</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Sum"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sum</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Min"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$min</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Max"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$max</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Avg"; $records; 0) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$avg</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Text"; $records; "" /* firstRow */; "" /* lastRow */; "" /* firstCol */; "" /* lastCol */; ", " /* rowSep */) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$text</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $records ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Sum: " & $sum & ¶ & "Min: " & $min & ¶ & "Max: " & $max & ¶ & "Avg: " & $avg & ¶ & "text: " & $text </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation/>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.Release
functions.description: Releases memory used for the SQL result.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

examples.label: Run command and free it later:

$ref
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "SELECT * FROM ProductImages FETCH FIRST 1 ROWS ONLY") ]
# ...
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.Release";
$ref
) ]
examples.xml_code:

    <Text> ...</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $ref ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.RowCount
functions.description: Queries number of rows in the result.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

examples.label: Query some values:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "select * from Clients")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["SQL error";
$sql
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["SQL result"; "got " &
MBS
( "FM.SQL.RowCount";
$sql
) &" rows with each " &
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.FieldCount
";
$sql
) & " …"]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql
)]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "select * from Clients")</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation>MBS("IsError")</Calculation>
  </Step>

    <Message>
      <Calculation>$sql</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="69" name="Else">
    <Restore state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation>"SQL result"; "got " & MBS( "FM.SQL.RowCount"; $sql ) &" rows with each " & MBS( "FM.SQL.FieldCount"</Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>$sql ) & " …"</Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $sql )</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.SQL.Sum
functions.description: Calculates sum value in a record set. You got a record set in memory and we can walk over a column in a given row range to add the sum value.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: Column
parameters.description: The index of the column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

examples.label: Query once and make several calculations:
examples.text_code:

Set Variable [
$Records
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; Get(FileName); "SELECT Price FROM Produkte") ]
# Now get sum, min, max and avg

$sum

( "FM.SQL.Sum";
$records
; 0) ]

FM.SQL.Min
";
$records
; 0) ]

FM.SQL.Max
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$avg
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Avg
";
$records
; 0) ]
Set Variable [
$text
; Value:

FM.SQL.Text
";
$records
; "" /* firstRow */; "" /* lastRow */; "" /* firstCol */; "" /* lastCol */; ", " /* rowSep */) ]
Set Variable [

; Value:

";
$records

$sum

& ¶ & "Max: " &
$max

functions.name: FM.SQL.Text
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.Text"; SQLref { ; FirstRow; LastRow; firstCol; lastCol; rowSeparator; colSeparator } )
functions.description: Queries text of SQL result. You can use this method to quickly get all fields in a given area as text.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

parameters.name: firstCol
parameters.description: The index of first column. Default is 0.

parameters.name: lastCol
parameters.description: The index of last column. Default is FM.SQL.FieldCount -1.

parameters.name: rowSeparator
parameters.description: The row separator. Default is CR. Can be more than one character.

parameters.name: colSeparator
parameters.description: The column separator. Default is tab character. Can be more than one character.

examples.label: Query fields and export as text and CSV:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "select * from Clients")]
If [
MBS
("IsError")]
Show Custom Dialog ["SQL error";
$sql
]
Else
Show Custom Dialog ["SQL result"; "got " &
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.RowCount
";
$sql
) &" rows with each " &
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.FieldCount
";
$sql
) & " …"]
# Write to text file

$text
; Value:

( "FM.SQL.Text";
$sql
)]
Set Variable [
$desktopFolder
; Value:

( "
Folders.UserDesktop
" )]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:

Path.AddPathComponent
";
$desktopFolder
; "fields.txt" )]
Set Variable [

";
$text
;
$path
; "UTF8")]

FM.SQL.CSV
";
$sql
)]

( "
Path.AddPathComponent
";
$desktopFolder
; "fields.csv" )]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.WriteTextFile
";
$text
;
$path
; "UTF8")]
# Cleanup
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql
)]
End If

functions.name: FM.SQL.ToMatrix
functions.description: Copies from result set to a Matrix. Allows you to do a FM.SQL.Execute , work with the result set, convert to matrix and then use matrix functions.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

functions.result: Returns matrix reference number or error.

functions.name: FM.SQL.XMLRecord
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.XMLRecord"; SQLref; RecordNodeName; Row; FieldNames { ; Flags } )
functions.description: Returns a row as XML object. You provide list of field names, which should be in same order as fields in SQL result. Optionally we can return SQL dates, times and timestamps as SQL format. Containers are returned as Base64 encoded data.

parameters.name: SQLref
parameters.description: The reference number returned by FM.SQL.Execute function.

parameters.name: RecordNodeName
parameters.description: The name for the parent XML nodes for ome record. Defaults to "record".

parameters.name: Row
parameters.description: The row you like to get. Zero based.

parameters.name: FieldNames
parameters.description: A list of field names for the XML.

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the XML creation. Pass 1 to get all values as text. Pass 2 to get all dates, times and timestamps in SQL format.

examples.label: Query records as XML:
examples.text_code:
# run query to get some data
Set Variable [
$sql
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "SELECT \"First\", \"Last\", Company, \"Photo Container\" FROM Contacts") ]
# query data
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.XMLRecord";
$sql
; "person"; 0; "First¶Last¶Company¶Photo"; 2) ]
# store result
Set Field [
Contacts::Output
;
$xml
]
# free memory
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql
) ]

functions.name: FM.SQL.XMLRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.SQL.XMLRecords"; SQLref; RootNodeName; RecordNodeName; FieldNames { ; Flags; FirstRow; LastRow } )
functions.description: Returns rows as XML. You provide list of field names, which should be in same order as fields in SQL result. Optionally we can return SQL dates, times and timestamps as SQL format. Containers are returned as Base64 encoded data.

parameters.name: RootNodeName

parameters.name: RecordNodeName

parameters.name: FieldNames

parameters.name: Flags
parameters.description: The flags for the XML creation. Pass 1 to get all values as text. Pass 2 to get all dates, times and timestamps in SQL format.

parameters.name: FirstRow
parameters.description: The index of first row. Default is 0.

parameters.name: LastRow
parameters.description: The index of last row. Default is FM.SQL.RowCount -1.

examples.label: Query contacts with related phone records as XML:
examples.text_code:
# Run query to fetch some records
Set Variable [
$sql1
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "SELECT \"PrimaryKey\", \"First Name\", \"Last Name\", \"Company\", \"Title\" FROM Contacts") ]
# get them as XML
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.XMLRecords";
$sql1
; "people"; "person"; "ID¶First¶Last¶Company¶Title"; 2+1) ]
#
# loop over records to look for related record
Set Variable [
$count
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.RowCount
";
$sql1
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 0 ]
If [
$index
≤
$count
]
Loop
# get primary key from result
Set Variable [
$key
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Field
";
$sql1
;
$index
; 0 ) ]
# Run query for related records
Set Variable [
$sql2
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Execute
"; ""; "SELECT Type, Number FROM \"Phone Numbers\" WHERE ForeignKey = ?";
$key
) ]
# get them as XML
Set Variable [
$xml2
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.SQL.XMLRecords";
$sql2
; "phones"; "phone"; "Type¶Number"; 2+1) ]
# Now insert them in the person node as new child phones
Set Variable [
$xml
; Value:
MBS
( "
XML.SetPathXML
";
$xml
; "people.person[" &
$index
&"].phones"; 0;
$xml2
) ]
# don't forget to release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql2
) ]
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
≥
$count
]
End Loop
End If
#
# don't forget to release memory
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.SQL.Release
";
$sql1
) ]
# Output the XML with format & color
Set Field [
Contacts::XML
;
MBS
("
XML.Colorize
";
MBS
("
XML.Format
";
$xml
)) ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Run query to fetch some records</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "SELECT \"PrimaryKey\", \"First Name\", \"Last Name\", \"Company\", \"Title\" FROM Contacts") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql1</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get them as XML</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.XMLRecords"; $sql1; "people"; "person"; "ID¶First¶Last¶Company¶Title"; 2+1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> loop over records to look for related record</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.RowCount"; $sql1 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>0 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $index ≤ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Always"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get primary key from result</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Field"; $sql1; $index; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$key</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Run query for related records</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Execute"; ""; "SELECT Type, Number FROM \"Phone Numbers\" WHERE ForeignKey = ?"; $key) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$sql2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> get them as XML</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.XMLRecords"; $sql2; "phones"; "phone"; "Type¶Number"; 2+1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml2</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Now insert them in the person node as new child phones</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "XML.SetPathXML"; $xml; "people.person[" & $index &"].phones"; 0; $xml2 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$xml</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> don't forget to release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $sql2) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index ≥ $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> don't forget to release memory</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.SQL.Release"; $sql1) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> Output the XML with format & color</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>MBS("XML.Colorize"; MBS("XML.Format"; $xml)) </Calculation>
    <Field table="Contacts" id="99999" name="XML "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: FM.TableStatistics
functions.description: Queries statistics on a table. The plugin will walk over all records and count fields with various data types and how many empty fields are there. Skips global and formula fields. We sum up the length of all text in text fields as well as the text content of all fields. We sum up the size of all internal containers, so ContainerByteSize + AllTextLengths*2 plus some metadata is the size of the database file. For internal containers we also count the streams inside, so you know how many JPEGs and other file types are in the table. External containers in this function are these references by a file path. Containers stored external, but managed by FileMaker are considered internal ones from the point of view of the plugin. e.g. {

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The target database name. Can be empty to not limit query to one database.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to check.

  </Step>

examples.label: Loop over all tables to find their sizes:
examples.text_code:
Go to Layout [ “TableStatistics” (TableStatistics) ; Animation: None ]
#
Set Variable [
$FileName
; Value: Get(FileName) ]
Set Variable [
$TableNames
; Value:
MBS
( "
FM.QueryBaseTableNames
";
$fileName
) ]
#
# loop counting up from 1 to
$count
Set Variable [
$count
; Value: ValueCount(
$TableNames
) ]
Set Variable [
$index
; Value: 1 ]
#
Loop [ Flush: Defer ]
# your script steps here
Set Variable [
$TableName
; Value: GetValue(
$tableNames
;
$index
) ]
Set Variable [
$JSON
; Value:
MBS
( "FM.TableStatistics";
$FileName
;
$TableName
) ]
#
New Record/Request
Set Field [
TableStatistics::JSON
;
$JSON
]
Set Field [
TableStatistics::Name
;
$TableName
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
#
# next
Set Variable [
$index
; Value:
$index
+ 1 ]
Exit Loop If [
$index
>
$count
]
End Loop

  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>ValueCount($TableNames) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$count</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="71" name="Loop">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <FlushType value="Defer"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> your script steps here</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetValue($tableNames; $index) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$TableName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "FM.TableStatistics"; $FileName; $TableName ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$JSON</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="7" name="New Record/Request"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$JSON </Calculation>
    <Field table="TableStatistics" id="99999" name="JSON "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$TableName </Calculation>
    <Field table="TableStatistics" id="99999" name="Name "/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="75" name="Commit Records/Requests">
    <NoInteract state="True"/>
    <Option state="False"/>
    <ESSForceCommit state="False"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)">
    <Text> next</Text>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>$index + 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$index</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="72" name="Exit Loop If">
    <Calculation> $index > $count </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="73" name="End Loop"/>

functions.name: FM.UpdateRecord
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.UpdateRecord"; FileName; TableName; IDField; IDValue; FieldName...; FieldValue... )
functions.description: Update an existing record in a table in one line. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must be unique. Then you pass one pair of field name and value for each field you like to like to change in the record. Please note that FM.UpdateRecord and FM.UpdateRecords are nearly identical. Just FM.UpdateRecord provides the check to make sure only one record matches while FM.UpdateRecords allows several records to be updated. So with FM.UpdateRecords you can easily erase a lot of data if you pass wrong ID! With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. The SQL functions in FileMaker do not trigger OnWindowsTransaction script trigger. If the record is locked, you get an error like this: [MBS] ERROR:  (301): Record is locked by another user. Your script may then wait and try again later. Does not take part in transactions in FileMaker. Changes are made directly to the database, so you can't revert them later with Revert Transaction script step. Please consider using newer JSON.UpdateRecord and JSON.UpdateRecords functions as they take a variable number of primary keys. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat FieldName and FieldValue parameters as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files. Using a table in another database file may require you adding the other file as an external data source and adding the external table to your relationship graph to grant access.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: IDField
parameters.description: The name of the field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ROWID for the record ID. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue
parameters.description: The value for the ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

parameters.name: FieldName...
parameters.description: A field name to set. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldValue...
parameters.description: A field value to use for setting the field in the parameter before. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

examples.label: Update a record:
examples.text_code:
MBS
( "FM.UpdateRecord";
""; "Asset Management"; /* file and table name */
"Asset ID"; "1"; /* ID field and value */
"Information"; "Just a test"; /* first field and value */
"Category"; "BestItems" ) /* second field and value */

""; "Asset Management"; /* file and table name */

"Information"; "Just a test"; /* first field and value */
"Category"; "BestItems" ) /* second field and value */</Calculation>

functions.name: FM.UpdateRecords
functions.signature: MBS( "FM.UpdateRecords"; FileName; TableName; IDField; IDValue; FieldName...; FieldValue... )
functions.description: Updates existing records in a table in one line. You pass to this function table name (and optional filename). To identify the record you pass the name of the ID field and the value for the record. The ID field must not be unique, so several records can be updated at once. Then you pass one pair of field name and value for each field you like to set in the new record. Please note that FM.UpdateRecord and FM.UpdateRecords are nearly identical. Just FM.UpdateRecord provides the check to make sure only one record matches while FM.UpdateRecords allows several records to be updated. So with FM.UpdateRecords you can easily erase a lot of data if you pass wrong ID! With plugin version 7.2 or later, you can specify fields and table via IDs and the plugin will lookup them for you at runtime. Table IDs and table names must be unique across all open files for this to work correctly. With plugin version 8.4 or newer, you can use GetFieldName() function to query field names and the plugin removes the table prefix with :: in the field name before passing it to SQL engine. You can use FM.ExecuteSQL.LastSQL function to see later what SQL was created and run. Please consider using newer JSON.UpdateRecord and JSON.UpdateRecords functions as they take a variable number of primary keys. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat FieldName and FieldValue parameters as often as you need.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name of where the table is inside. Can be empty to look for the table in all files.

parameters.name: TableName
parameters.description: The name of the table to insert record into. Can be ID of table, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove field name automatically.

parameters.name: IDField
parameters.description: The name of the field which provides an unique ID for each record, so we can identify the field. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: IDValue
parameters.description: The value for the ID field to identify the record. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

parameters.name: FieldName...
parameters.description: A field name to set. Can be ID of field, so we lookup name by ID. Can be result of GetFieldName() function as we remove table name automatically.

parameters.name: FieldValue...
parameters.description: A field value to use for setting the field in the parameter before. Data type of parameter must match the data type of the field.

functions.name: GetSystemUIMode
functions.description: Queries the current system UI mode. See the SetSystemUIMode function for details on the possible values.

functions.name: ImagePicker.AvailableCaptureModesForCameraDevice
functions.description: Queries list of available modes. List contains Photo if photo is available and video if video is available. This function is free to use. Created 17th June 2017 , last changed 18th June 2017 ImageCapture.cancelScan - ImagePicker.AvailableMediaTypesForSourceType 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: CameraDevice
parameters.description: The new setting. Can be front or rear.

functions.name: ImagePicker.AvailableMediaTypesForSourceType
functions.description: Returns an array of the available media types for the specified source type. Some iOS devices support video recording. Use this method, along with the ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable function, to determine if video recording is available on a device. The availability of video recording is indicated by the presence of the kUTTypeMovie (public.movie) media type for the camera source type.

parameters.name: sourceType
parameters.description: The source to use to pick an image. Can be PhotoLibrary, Camera or SavedPhotosAlbum.

examples.label: Check availability:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$CameraAvailable
; Value:
MBS
("
ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable
"; "Camera") ]
Set Variable [
$PhotoLibraryAvailable
; Value:
MBS
("
ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable
"; "PhotoLibrary") ]
Set Variable [
$SavedPhotosAlbumAvailable
; Value:
MBS
("
ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable
"; "SavedPhotosAlbum") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable"; "Camera") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CameraAvailable</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable"; "PhotoLibrary") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PhotoLibraryAvailable</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS("ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable"; "SavedPhotosAlbum") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetAllowsEditing
functions.description: Gets whether editing is allowed. A Boolean value indicating whether the user is allowed to edit a selected still image or movie. This property is set to 0 by default.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetCameraCaptureMode
functions.description: Queries the capture mode. The capture mode used by the camera.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetCameraDevice
functions.description: Queries camera device. The camera used by the image picker controller. Default is Rear.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetCameraFlashMode
functions.description: Queries flash mode. The flash mode used by the active camera. The value of this property specifies the behavior of the still-image flash when the value of the cameraCaptureMode property is photo, and specifies the behavior of the video torch when cameraCaptureMode is video.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetEvaluate
functions.description: Queries the evaluation to run when email composer is done.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetFileName
functions.description: Queries the file name of the script to run when email composer is done.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetScriptName
functions.description: Queries the script name of the script to run when email composer is done.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetShowsCameraControls
functions.description: Queries camera controls visibility. Indicates whether the image picker displays the default camera controls. The default value of this property is 1, which specifies that the default camera controls are visible in the picker. Set it to 0 to hide the default controls if you want to instead provide a custom overlay view using the cameraOverlayView property. If you set this property to 0 and provide your own custom controls, you can take multiple pictures before dismissing the image picker interface. However, if you set this property to 1, your delegate must dismiss the image picker interface after the user takes one picture or cancels the operation. You can access this property only when the source type of the image picker is set to Camera. Attempting to access this property for other source types results in an error. Depending on the value you assign to the mediaTypes property, the default controls display the still camera or movie camera interface, or a selection control that lets the user choose the picker interface.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetSourceType
functions.description: Gets the source type. The type of picker interface to be displayed by the controller. Prior to running the picker interface, set this value to the desired source type. The source type you set must be available and an error will be returned if it is not. If you change this property while the picker is visible, the picker interface changes to match the new value in this property. The default value is PhotoLibrary.

functions.name: ImagePicker.GetVideoMaximumDuration
functions.description: Queries maximum duration. The maximum duration, in seconds, for a video recording. The default value for this property is 10 minutes (600 seconds). When a user taps the Share button to send a movie to MMS, MobileMe, YouTube, or another destination, an appropriate duration limit and an appropriate video quality are enforced. This property is available only if the mediaTypes property’s value array includes the kUTTypeMovie (public.movie) media type.

functions.name: ImagePicker.HasMedia
functions.description: Queries whether media exists. Returns 1 if there was an image or video selected, else 0.

functions.name: ImagePicker.IsCameraDeviceAvailable
functions.description: Checks camera availability. Returns a Boolean value that indicates whether a given camera is available.

parameters.name: CameraDevice
parameters.description: The new setting. Can be front or rear.

functions.name: ImagePicker.IsFlashAvailableForCameraDevice
functions.description: Checks flash availability. Indicates whether a given camera has flash illumination capability. Returns 1 if flash is available or 0 if not. This function is free to use. Created 17th June 2017 , last changed 18th June 2017 ImagePicker.IsCameraDeviceAvailable - ImagePicker.IsPresented 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

parameters.name: CameraDevice
parameters.description: Which camera to check. Can be front or rear.

functions.name: ImagePicker.IsPresented
functions.description: Checks whether panel is displayed. Returns 1 if visible and 0 if not.

functions.name: ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable
functions.description: Check for source availability. Returns a Boolean value indicating whether the device supports picking media using the specified source type. 1 if the device supports the specified source type; 0 if the specified source type is not available. Because a media source may not be present or may be unavailable, devices may not always support all source types. For example, if you attempt to pick an image from the user’s library and the library is empty, this method returns 0. Similarly, if the camera is already in use, this method returns 0. Before attempting to use an ImagePickerController object to pick an image, you must call this method to ensure that the desired source type is available.

parameters.name: sourceType
parameters.description: The source to use to pick an image. Can be PhotoLibrary, Camera or SavedPhotosAlbum.

examples.label: Check for source type being available:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$SourceType
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable";
$sourceType
) ]
If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Can't pick picture." ; "The source " &
$sourceType
& " is not available." ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "failed" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$SourceType</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "ImagePicker.IsSourceTypeAvailable"; $sourceType ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Can't pick picture." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"The source " & $sourceType & " is not available." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: "failed" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

examples.label: Store image:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "
ImagePicker.HasMedia
" ) ]
New Record/Request
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "
ImagePicker.MediaEditedImage
"; "PNG") ]
If [ IsEmpty (
$image
) ]
Set Variable [
$Image
; Value:
MBS
( "ImagePicker.MediaOriginalImage"; "PNG") ]
End If
Set Field [
ImagePicker::Picture
;
$image
]
Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "ImagePicker.HasMedia" ) ] 
	New Record/Request
	Set Variable [ $Image ; Value: MBS( "ImagePicker.MediaEditedImage"; "PNG") ] 
	If [ IsEmpty ( $image ) ] 
		Set Variable [ $Image ; Value: MBS( "ImagePicker.MediaOriginalImage"; "PNG") ] 
	End If
	Set Field [ ImagePicker::Picture ; $image ] 
	Commit Records/Requests [ With dialog: Off ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImagePicker.MediaPath

Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

functions.name: ImagePicker.MediaReferenceURL

functions.name: ImagePicker.Present
functions.description: Presents the picker view. This will show the user interface and start the process. Your script may call this in a script and then wait for the script trigger. You may decide whether you close it by calling ImagePicker.Dismiss yourself or you leave it open to take more than one picture.

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetAllowsEditing
functions.description: Sets whether editing is allowed. A Boolean value indicating whether the user is allowed to edit a selected still image or movie. This property is set to 0 by default.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to enable or 0 to disable.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetCameraCaptureMode
functions.description: Sets the capture mode. The capture mode used by the camera.

parameters.name: Mode
parameters.description: The mode. Can be Photo or Video.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetCameraDevice
functions.description: Sets camera device. The camera used by the image picker controller. Default is Rear.

parameters.name: CameraDevice
parameters.description: The new setting. Can be front or rear.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetCameraFlashMode
functions.description: Sets flash mode. The flash mode used by the active camera. The value of this property specifies the behavior of the still-image flash when the value of the cameraCaptureMode property is photo, and specifies the behavior of the video torch when cameraCaptureMode is video.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Can be On, Off and Auto.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to call when composer is done. Parameter may be "Cancel" if user canceled the dialog or "Image" if something was picked.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetMediaTypes
functions.description: Sets the list of media types to access. Depending on the media types you assign to this property, the picker displays a dedicated interface for still images or movies, or a selection control that lets the user choose the picker interface. Before setting this property, check which media types are available by calling the ImagePicker.AvailableMediaTypesForSourceType function. If you set this property to an empty list, or to an list in which none of the media types is available for the current source, the system returns an error. When capturing media, the value of this property determines the camera interface to display. When browsing saved media, this property determines the types of media presented in the interface. By default, the value of this property is the image identifier, which designates the still camera interface when capturing media, and specifies that only still images should be displayed in the media picker when browsing saved media. The following example shows how to designate the movie capture interface, or to indicate that only movies should be displayed when browsing saved media. You may just query ImagePicker.AvailableMediaTypesForSourceType for the media types.

parameters.name: MediaTypes
parameters.description: The media types to request.

ImagePicker.SetSourceType

Set Variable [
$r

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetScript
functions.description: Sets the script to call when picker is done. Parameter may be "Cancel" if user canceled the dialog or "Image" if something was picked. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script to trigger.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name of the script to launch.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetShowsCameraControls
functions.description: Sets camera controls visibility. Indicates whether the image picker displays the default camera controls. The default value of this property is 1, which specifies that the default camera controls are visible in the picker. Set it to 0 to hide the default controls if you want to instead provide a custom overlay view using the cameraOverlayView property. If you set this property to 0 and provide your own custom controls, you can take multiple pictures before dismissing the image picker interface. However, if you set this property to 1, your delegate must dismiss the image picker interface after the user takes one picture or cancels the operation. You can access this property only when the source type of the image picker is set to Camera. Attempting to access this property for other source types results in an error. Depending on the value you assign to the mediaTypes property, the default controls display the still camera or movie camera interface, or a selection control that lets the user choose the picker interface.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new setting. Pass 1 to show camera controls or 0 to hide.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetSourceType
functions.description: Sets the source type. The type of picker interface to be displayed by the controller. Prior to running the picker interface, set this value to the desired source type. The source type you set must be available and an error will be returned if it is not. If you change this property while the picker is visible, the picker interface changes to match the new value in this property. The default value is PhotoLibrary.

parameters.name: sourceType
parameters.description: The source to use to pick an image. Can be PhotoLibrary, Camera or SavedPhotosAlbum.

functions.name: ImagePicker.SetVideoMaximumDuration
functions.description: Sets maximum duration. The maximum duration, in seconds, for a video recording. The default value for this property is 10 minutes (600 seconds). When a user taps the Share button to send a movie to MMS, MobileMe, YouTube, or another destination, an appropriate duration limit and an appropriate video quality are enforced. This property is available only if the mediaTypes property’s value array includes the kUTTypeMovie (public.movie) media type.

parameters.name: Value
parameters.description: The new maximum length in seconds.

functions.name: ImagePicker.StartVideoCapture
functions.description: Starts video capture. Select the camera via ImagePicker.SetCameraDevice function. Returns OK on success or "Failed" on failure. This method may return a value of "Failed" for various reasons, among them the following: Movie capture is already in progress The device does not support movie capture The device is out of disk space Use this function in conjunction to initiate the programmatic capture of a movie. You can take more than one movie without leaving the interface, but to do so requires you to hide the default image picker controls. Calling this method while a movie is being captured has no effect. You must call the ImagePicker.StopVideoCapture function, and then wait until the associated script trigger is run, before you can capture another movie. Calling this method when the source type of the image picker is set to a value other than Camera results in an error.

functions.name: ImagePicker.StopVideoCapture
functions.description: Stops video capture. After you call this method to stop video capture, the system calls the script trigger.

functions.name: ImagePicker.TakePicture
functions.description: Captures a still image using the camera. Use this method in conjunction to initiate the programmatic capture of a still image. This supports taking more than one picture without leaving the interface, but requires that you hide the default image picker controls. Calling this method while an image is being captured has no effect. You must wait until the script trigger is run before you can capture another picture. Calling this method when the source type of the image picker is set to a value other than Camera results in an error.

functions.name: ImagePicker.VideoIsCompatibleWithSavedPhotosAlbum
functions.description: Checks video. Is a specific video eligible to be saved to the saved photos album? This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The file path to the video file.

functions.name: ImagePicker.WriteImageToSavedPhotosAlbum
functions.description: Adds a photo to the saved photos album (camera roll). The work is done in background and we can optionally trigger a script when done. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Image
parameters.description: The image container to add to photo roll.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name for the trigger.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The script parameter to use.

examples.label: Add image to saved photos:
examples.text_code:
If [ IsEmpty (
ImagePicker::Picture
) ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "No image?" ]
Else
Set Variable [
$path
; Value:
MBS
( "ImagePicker.WriteImageToSavedPhotosAlbum";
ImagePicker::Picture
; Get(FileName); "AddedToCameraRoll" ) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ IsEmpty ( ImagePicker::Picture ) ] 
	Show Custom Dialog [ "No image?" ] 
Else
	Set Variable [ $path ; Value: MBS( "ImagePicker.WriteImageToSavedPhotosAlbum"; ImagePicker::Picture; Get(FileName); "AddedToCameraRoll" ) ] 
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: ImagePicker.WriteVideoToSavedPhotosAlbum
functions.description: Adds a video to the saved photos album. The work is done in background and we can optionally trigger a script when done. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: FilePath
parameters.description: The file path to the video file.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name for the trigger.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The script parameter to use.

functions.name: IsIOSSimulator
functions.description: Queries whether run on iOS simulator. Returns 0 on iOS Device or on macOS, Windows and Linux.

examples.label: Distinguish between device and simulator:
examples.text_code:
If [
MBS
( "IsIOSDevice" ) = 1 ]
# iOS specific things for device
Else If [
MBS
("IsiOSSimulator") = 1 ]
# iOS specific things for simulator
Else
# other cases
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [ MBS( "IsIOSDevice" ) = 1 ] 
	# iOS specific things for device
Else If [ MBS("IsiOSSimulator") = 1 ] 
	# iOS specific things for simulator
Else
	# other cases
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MailComposer.AddAttachmentContainer
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a container field. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments.

parameters.name: Container
parameters.description: The container to attach to email.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "application/octet-stream".

examples.label: Adds an attachment:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$FieldName
; Value: Get(ScriptParameter) ]
Set Variable [
$Container
; Value: GetField (
$FieldName
) ]
If [ IsEmpty (
$container
) = 0 ]
Set Variable [
$path
; Value: GetAsText (
$Container
) ]
Set Variable [
$name
; Value:
MBS
( "
Path.LastPathComponent
";
$path
) ]
Set Variable [
$extension
; Value:
MBS
( "
Files.FileExtension
";
$name
) ]
Set Variable [
$mimeType
; Value:
MBS
( "
Text.FileExtensionToMimeType
";
$extension
) ]
#
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MailComposer.AddAttachmentContainer";
$container
;
$name
;
$mimeType
) ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Get(ScriptParameter) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$FieldName</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetField ( $FieldName) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$Container</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> IsEmpty ( $container ) = 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>GetAsText ( $Container ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$path</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Path.LastPathComponent"; $path) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$name</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Files.FileExtension"; $name ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$extension</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Text.FileExtensionToMimeType"; $extension ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$mimeType</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="89" name="# (comment)"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.AddAttachmentContainer"; $container; $name; $mimeType) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MailComposer.AddAttachmentFile
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a file path. Attachments over 100 MB are not supported. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to include as attachment.

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "application/octet-stream".

functions.name: MailComposer.AddAttachmentText
functions.description: Adds an attachment to the email based on a text. Attachments over 100 MB are not supported. You can use Text.FileExtensionToMimeType to find the mime type for a file extension. You can use AddAttachment several times to add several attachments.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to use for the attachment.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for text parameter. Default is UTF-8. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

parameters.name: Name
parameters.description: The name for the attachment. If empty, version 6.2 or newer uses file name from file path.

parameters.name: MimeType
parameters.description: The mime type to use. e.g. "application/pdf" for PDF files. If missing, we use "text/plain".

functions.name: MailComposer.CanSendMail
functions.description: Whether emails can be send. Returns 1 if the user has set up the device for sending email. The client may continue to set the recipients and content if the return value was 1.  If 0 was the result, the client has a couple options.  It may choose to simply notify the user of the inability to send mail, or it may issue a "mailto" URL via Open URL. This function can be called before having a composer initialized.

examples.label: Checks whether we can email:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MailComposer.CanSendMail" ) ]
If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Can't send emails." ; "This iOS device is not configured to send emails." ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "failed" ]
End If
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.CanSendMail" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Can't send emails." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"This iOS device is not configured to send emails." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: "failed" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MailComposer.Dismiss
functions.description: Dismisses the dialog. Something you can call in a script to cancel the email creation.

functions.name: MailComposer.Error

</fmxmlsnippet>
