functions.name: MailComposer.Present
functions.description: Presents the email composer view. This will show the user interface and start the process. Your script may call this in a script and than wait for the script trigger.

examples.label: Setups email and send:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.CanSendMail
" ) ]
If [
$r
≠ 1 ]
Show Custom Dialog [ "Can't send emails." ; "This iOS device is not configured to send emails." ]
Exit Script [ Text Result: "failed" ]
End If
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.New
" ) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.SetSubject
";
PlaceHolder::Subject
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.SetScript
"; Get(FileName); "Sent") ]
If [ Length (
PlaceHolder::Body
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.SetMessageBody
";
PlaceHolder::Body
; Position (
PlaceHolder::Body
; "<html>" ; 1 ; 1 ) > 0 ) ]
End If
If [ Length (
PlaceHolder::CCRecipients
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.SetCCRecipients
";
PlaceHolder::CCRecipients
) ]
End If
If [ Length (
PlaceHolder::BCCRecipients
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.SetBCCRecipients
";
PlaceHolder::BCCRecipients
) ]
End If
If [ Length (
PlaceHolder::TORecipients
) > 0 ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.SetTORecipients
";
PlaceHolder::TORecipients
) ]
End If
Perform Script [ “AddAttachment” ; Parameter: GetFieldName (
PlaceHolder::Attachment1
) ]
Perform Script [ “AddAttachment” ; Parameter: GetFieldName (
PlaceHolder::Attachment2
) ]
Perform Script [ “AddAttachment” ; Parameter: GetFieldName (
PlaceHolder::Attachment3
) ]
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MailComposer.Present") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.CanSendMail" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> $r ≠ 1 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="87" name="Show Custom Dialog">
    <Title>
      <Calculation> "Can't send emails." </Calculation>
    </Title>
    <Message>
      <Calculation>"This iOS device is not configured to send emails." </Calculation>
    </Message>
    <Buttons>
      <Button CommitState="True">
        <Calculation>"OK"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False">
        <Calculation>"Cancel"</Calculation>
      </Button>
      <Button CommitState="False"/>
    </Buttons>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="103" name="Exit Script">
    <Calculation> Text Result: "failed" </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.New" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.SetSubject"; PlaceHolder::Subject ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.SetScript"; Get(FileName); "Sent") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( PlaceHolder::Body ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.SetMessageBody"; PlaceHolder::Body; Position ( PlaceHolder::Body ; "<html>" ; 1 ; 1 ) > 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( PlaceHolder::CCRecipients ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.SetCCRecipients"; PlaceHolder::CCRecipients) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( PlaceHolder::BCCRecipients ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.SetBCCRecipients"; PlaceHolder::BCCRecipients) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="68" name="If">
    <Restore state="False"/>
    <Calculation> Length ( PlaceHolder::TORecipients ) > 0 </Calculation>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.SetTORecipients"; PlaceHolder::TORecipients) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="70" name="End If"/>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>GetFieldName ( PlaceHolder::Attachment1 ) </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="AddAttachment"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>GetFieldName ( PlaceHolder::Attachment2 ) </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="AddAttachment"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="1" name="Perform Script">
    <Calculation>GetFieldName ( PlaceHolder::Attachment3 ) </Calculation>
    <Script id="99999" name="AddAttachment"/>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.Present") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MailComposer.Result
functions.description: Queries result of email composer. If email composer is not yet finished, the result is empty. Status can be Cancelled, Saved, Sent, Failed.

examples.label: Show result in a field:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$result
; Value:
MBS
( "MailComposer.Result" ) ]
Set Variable [
$error
; Value:
MBS
( "
MailComposer.Error
" ) ]
Set Field [
PlaceHolder::Result
;
$result
& ": " &
$error
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.Result" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$result</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.Error" ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$error</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="76" name="Set Field">
    <Calculation>$result & ": " & $error </Calculation>
    <Field table="PlaceHolder" id="99999" name="Result "/>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MailComposer.SetBCCRecipients
functions.description: Sets the BCC header for the email message to the specified email addresses. Will set the BCC header for the email message.  This should be called prior to display. Recipient addresses should be specified as per RFC5322. After the view has been presented to the user, this method will no longer change the value.

parameters.name: Recipients
parameters.description: List of recipients.

functions.name: MailComposer.SetCCRecipients
functions.description: Sets the CC header for the email message to the specified email addresses. Will set the CC header for the email message.  This should be called prior to display. Recipient addresses should be specified as per RFC5322. After the view has been presented to the user, this method will no longer change the value.

parameters.name: Recipients
parameters.description: List of recipients.

functions.name: MailComposer.SetEvaluate
functions.description: Sets the expression to call when composer is done. In the script you can check with MailComposer.Result and MailComposer.Error what happened.

parameters.name: Expression
parameters.description: The expression to evaluate. Can be in local language of FileMaker Pro or english. For Server must always be in english.

functions.name: MailComposer.SetMessageBody
functions.description: Sets the body of the email message to the specified content. This method will set the body of the email message.  This should be called prior to display. The user's signature, if specified, will be added after the body content.

parameters.name: Body
parameters.description: The text containing the body contents of the email message.

parameters.name: IsHTML
parameters.description: A boolean value indicating if the body argument is to be interpreted as HTML content. Pass 1 if Body is html or 0 for plain text. Default is 0 for plain text.

functions.name: MailComposer.SetScript
functions.description: Sets the script to call when composer is done. In the script you can check with MailComposer.Result and MailComposer.Error what happened. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The file name for the script to trigger.

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The script name of the script to launch.

parameters.name: Parameter
parameters.description: The parameter to pass to the script.

examples.label: Set script:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$r
; Value:
MBS
( "MailComposer.SetScript"; Get(FileName); "Sent") ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "MailComposer.SetScript"; Get(FileName); "Sent") </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MailComposer.SetSubject
functions.description: Sets the Subject header for the email message. This method will set the Subject header for the email message.  This should be called prior to display. Newlines are removed from the parameter. After the view has been presented to the user, this method will no longer change the value.

parameters.name: Subject
parameters.description: The new subject.

functions.name: MailComposer.SetTORecipients
functions.description: Sets the To header for the email message to the specified email addresses. Set the To header for the email message.  This should be called prior to display. Recipient addresses should be specified as per RFC5322. After the view has been presented to the user, this method will no longer change the value.

parameters.name: Recipients
parameters.description: List of recipients.

functions.name: MouseCursor.Hide
functions.description: Hides the mouse cursor. As FileMaker may reset the cursor to be visible when scripts end, you may not see the mouse actually hide.

functions.name: MouseCursor.HideUntilMouseMoves
functions.description: Hides mouse until user moves it. Good for forms where mouse cursor is not needed and text input required.

$r

functions.name: MouseCursor.Set
functions.description: Sets the mouse cursor. As FileMaker loves to reset the cursor, you can't use it only in places where FileMaker does not reset it. Like in a script without pauses. Cursor names allowed on macOS: arrow IBeam pointingHand closedHand openHand resizeLeft resizeRight resizeLeftRight resizeUp resizeDown resizeUpDown crosshair disappearingItem With macOS 10.6 and newer you can also use this cursors: operationNotAllowed dragLink dragCopy contextualMenu Cursor names allowed on Windows: AppStarting Arrow IBeam Hand OpenHand resizeLeftRight resizeUpDown crosshair operationNotAllowed Help SizeNESW SizeNS SizeNWSE SizeWE SizeAll UpArrow Wait

parameters.name: CursorName
parameters.description: The name of cursor you'd like to set.

examples.label: Set hand cursor:

( "MouseCursor.Set"; "openHand" )
examples.xml_code:

</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: MouseCursor.Show
functions.description: Shows the mouse cursor.

functions.name: System.Logout
functions.description: Logout current user. It is possible that user or other application stops the logout process. Operation will run asynchronously. The function returns immediately and logout process starts a second later.

functions.name: System.Restart
functions.description: Restarts computer. It is possible that user or other application stops the shutdown process. Operation will run asynchronously. The function returns immediately and shutdown process starts a second later.

functions.name: System.Shutdown
functions.description: Shuts computer down. It is possible that user or other application stops the shutdown process. Operation will run asynchronously. The function returns immediately and shutdown process starts a second later.

functions.name: System.Sleep
functions.description: Asks computer to go to sleep (standby). It is possible that user or other application stops the sleep process. Operation will run asynchronously. The function returns immediately and sleep starts a second later.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Index of the item from 0 to UnZipFile.ItemCount -1.

functions.name: UnZipFile.ItemComment
functions.description: Queries the file comment of the given item.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Index of the item from 0 to UnZipFile.ItemCount -1.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.result: Returns comment as text or error.

functions.name: UnZipFile.ItemCompressedSize
functions.description: Queries compressed item size.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Index of the item from 0 to UnZipFile.ItemCount -1.

functions.result: Returns size as number or error.

examples.label: Queries sizes:
examples.text_code:
Set Variable [
$UncompressedSize
; Value:
MBS
( "
UnZipFile.ItemUncompressedSize
"; 0 ) ]
Set Variable [
$CompressedSize
; Value:
MBS
( "UnZipFile.ItemCompressedSize"; 0 ) ]
Set Variable [
$PercentSaved
; Value: (
$UncompressedSize
-
$CompressedSize
) /
$UncompressedSize
]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "UnZipFile.ItemUncompressedSize"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$UncompressedSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "UnZipFile.ItemCompressedSize"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CompressedSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>($UncompressedSize - $CompressedSize) / $UncompressedSize </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PercentSaved</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: UnZipFile.ItemFlag
functions.description: Queries the flags for this item. The flag is a bit field with various values. Last bit of the flag is encryption state: value is 1 (odd) for encrypted and 0 (even) for unencrypted value. The bit values 2 and 4 define compression level between 0 to 9.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Index of the item from 0 to UnZipFile.ItemCount -1.

examples.text_code:

$compressed
; Value:
MBS
( "
Math.BitwiseAND
";
MBS
( "UnZipFile.ItemFlag"; Index ); 1 ) = 1 ]
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "Math.BitwiseAND"; MBS( "UnZipFile.ItemFlag"; Index ); 1 ) = 1 </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$compressed</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "UnZipFile.ItemUncompressedSize"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$UncompressedSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS( "UnZipFile.ItemCompressedSize"; 0 ) </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$CompressedSize</Name>
  </Step>
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>($UncompressedSize - $CompressedSize) / $UncompressedSize </Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$PercentSaved</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Index of the item from 0 to UnZipFile.ItemCount -1.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for result. Default is native. This function can also handle UTF-16 as well as UTF-16LE and UTF-16BE for little/big endian byte order. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: UnZipFile.ReadToFile
functions.description: Reads file from archive and writes it to a new file. If you want to decrypt an encrypted file, please use UnZipFile.UsePassword before calling this function. This function requires a native path. Use Path.FileMakerPathToNativePath to convert a FileMaker path to a native path if required. If you like to have the user choose the path, you can use FileDialog functions. For Server be aware that server has limited permissions and may not be able to access all files on a computer.

parameters.name: Index
parameters.description: Index of the item from 0 to UnZipFile.ItemCount -1.

parameters.name: Path
parameters.description: The native file path to the file to write from the archive.

functions.name: UnZipFile.UsePassword
functions.description: Sets the password to use for uncompressing. The password is needed to open encrypted files in archive. Each item in archive could have its own password, but normally all have the same.

parameters.name: Password
parameters.description: The password text.

parameters.name: Encoding
parameters.description: The text encoding for password parameter. Default is native. Possible encoding names: ANSI, ISO-8859-1, Latin1, Mac, Native, UTF-8, DOS, Hex, Base64 or Windows. More listed in the FAQ .

functions.name: URLScheme.GetDefaultHandler
functions.description: Queries the default scheme handler for the given scheme name. On Mac returns the bundle id. e.g. com.fetchsoftworks.Fetch for Fetch.app. On Windows returns command line for launching target app. e.g. '"C:\Program Files\Internet Explorer\iexplore.exe" %1'

parameters.name: scheme
parameters.description: The schema name.

functions.name: URLScheme.InstallURLEventHandler
functions.description: Installs an event handler for the URL event and calls the script you specify with the URL as parameter. Using this event you can register your application as the handler for an URL schema and get the URL if the user clicks a link for your schemas. This function is only useful in a locked FileMaker solution. Please check the Readme in the examples for more details. With plugin version 6.0 or newer the script name can be a script ID number. In that case the plugin queries the script name for the given script ID. This allows to call scripts by ID and avoid problems if scripts are later renamed. Notice: FileMaker 19.2 adds a fmplugin extended privileges. If you have such an extended privileges to allow the plugin to trigger scripts, you need to grant permissions for it. If such a privilege is not defined, the plugin is allowed to trigger scripts. See FileMaker product documentation for details.

parameters.name: FileName
parameters.description: The name of the FileMaker File that contains the script

parameters.name: ScriptName
parameters.description: The name of the script to run

functions.result: Returns OK or an error message.

examples.label: Installs a script:
examples.text_code:
MBS
("URLScheme.InstallURLEventHandler"; "MyDatabase.fp7"; "MyURLScript")
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">

    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: URLScheme.SetDefaultHandler
functions.description: Sets the user's preferred handler for the specified URL scheme to point to the current application. It's probably best to ask the user's permission before changing their default browser! With plugin version 5.3 this works on Windows if FileMaker or runtime is launched as admin. Still that may not help much as when FileMaker is launched with URL, it shows an error message.

parameters.name: scheme
parameters.description: The schema name.

parameters.name: BundleID
parameters.description: The bundle ID for the application. If empty, we use the one of the current application.

functions.result: Returns OK or an error message on failure.

# okay or failure?
If [

≠ "com.filemaker.client.runtime12.Test"]

" )]

; Value:

( "

"; "-f";
$path
/*"/Applications/Test/Test.app" */)]
Set Variable [

; Value:

( "
RunTask.Launch
")]
# wait until finished
Loop
Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
Exit Loop If [
MBS
("
RunTask.IsRunning
") ≠ 1]
End Loop

; Value:

examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>If [MBS("IsMacOSX") and MBS("IsRuntime")]
	# make us the default app for FMP URL scheme
	Set Variable [$vorher; Value:MBS( "URLScheme.GetDefaultHandler"; "fmp" )]
	Set Variable [$setResult; Value:MBS( "URLScheme.SetDefaultHandler"; "fmp" )]
	Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "URLScheme.GetDefaultHandler"; "fmp" )]
	
	# okay or failure?
	If [$r ≠ "com.filemaker.client.runtime12.Test"]
		# Register by using lsregister tool on command line
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "RunTask.NewTask" )]
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "RunTask.SetLaunchPath"; "/System/Library/Frameworks/CoreServices.framework/Versions/A/Frameworks/LaunchServices.framework/Versions/A/Support/lsregister")]
		Set Variable [$path; Value:MBS( "App.GetProcessPath" )]
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "RunTask.SetArguments"; "-f"; $path /*"/Applications/Test/Test.app" */)]
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "RunTask.Launch")]
		# wait until finished
		Loop
			Pause/Resume Script [Duration (seconds): ,1]
			Exit Loop If [MBS("RunTask.IsRunning") ≠ 1]
		End Loop
		# Check again
		Set Variable [$setResult; Value:MBS( "URLScheme.SetDefaultHandler"; "fmp" )]
		Set Variable [$r; Value:MBS( "URLScheme.GetDefaultHandler"; "fmp" )]
	End If
End If</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsScript.AddCode
functions.description: Adds the code to the specified script. Used to build a script in memory. You can use this function more then once on the same script. If you add code that overlaps with code that is already in the script then the last one added takes precedence. For Ex. IF you add FUNCTION HelloWorld() HelloWorld = "HELLO WORLD" END FUNCTION To a VBScript that already has the function "HelloWorld()" in it, it is replaced by your new version. If the code can't be added to the script one of the following is returned - "MBS: Missing ScriptID." - "MBS: Failed to create Windows Script object." - description of exception If you use VBScript, you don't need "dim" keyword for defining variables.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

parameters.name: Code
parameters.description: the code to add to the script

examples.label: Trigger a FileMaker Script Using OS Scripting
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
// --- the name of the script to run ------------------

ScriptName = "Triggered Script";
FileName = Get(FileName);

//------------------------------------------------------------

//--- don't need to edit anything below this line --------


Applescript = "do script " & Quote(ScriptName);

VBScriptText = 
"FUNCTION DoFMSCript(FileName, ScriptName)" & "¶" & 

"Set fmApp = CreateObject(\"FMPro.Application\")" & "¶" & 
"fmApp.Visible = True" & "¶" & 
"Set fmDocs = fmApp.Documents" & "¶" &
"For Each fmDoc In fmDocs" & "¶" &
"If InStr(LCase(fmDoc.fullname), LCase(FileName)) > 0 Then" & "¶" &
" fmDoc.dofmscript (ScriptName)" & "¶" &
"End If" & "¶" &
"Next" & "¶" &

"END FUNCTION";

ScriptID = MBS("WindowsScript.Create");
lang= MBS("WindowsScript.SetLanguage"; ScriptID; "VBScript");
addcode = MBS("WindowsScript.AddCode"; ScriptID; VBScriptText)

];
Case(
Get ( SystemPlatform ) = 1;

// Mac OSX Case
MBS( 
"Applescript.Run";
Applescript
);

// Windows Case
MBS("WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction";ScriptID; "DoFMScript";FileName; ScriptName) & 
MBS("WindowsScript.Close"; ScriptID)

)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

"End If" & "¶" &
"Next" & "¶" &
"END FUNCTION";
ScriptID =
MBS
("
WindowsScript.Create
");
lang=
MBS
("
WindowsScript.SetLanguage
"; ScriptID; "VBScript");
addcode =
MBS
("WindowsScript.AddCode"; ScriptID; VBScriptText)
];
Case(
Get ( SystemPlatform ) = 1;
// Mac OSX Case
MBS
(
"
AppleScript.Run
";
Applescript
);
// Windows Case
MBS
("
WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction
";ScriptID; "DoFMScript";theFileName; theScriptName) &
MBS
("
WindowsScript.Close
"; ScriptID)
)
)

examples.label: Use JScript to Get System Date
examples.xml_code:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<fmxmlsnippet type="FMObjectList">
  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>Let(
[
JScriptText = "function TestDate(){var d =new Date();
return d.toGMTString();}";
scriptID = MBS("WindowsScript.Create");
SetLang = MBS("WindowsScript.SetLanguage";1;"JScript");
addcode = MBS("WindowsScript.AddCode";1;JScriptText);
theDate = MBS("WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction";1;"TestDate");
closeScript = MBS("WindowsScript.Close";1)

];

theDate

)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

  <Step enable="True" id="141" name="Set Variable">
    <Value>
      <Calculation>MBS_TriggerScript ( "Triggered Script" ; Get ( FileName ) ) 


Custom Function Definition
/*###############################################

MBS_TriggerScript
created 10/26/06, by Todd Geist, todd@geistinteractive.com

Parameters: theScriptName, theFileName

Dependancies: MBS FileMaker Plug-in.

Notes: Uses VBScript and Applescript to run a script

################################################*/
Let(
[

Applescript = "do script " & Quote(theScriptName);

VBScriptText = 
"FUNCTION DoFMSCript(FileName, ScriptName)" & "¶" & 

"Set fmApp = CreateObject(\"FMPro.Application\")" & "¶" & 
"fmApp.Visible = True" & "¶" & 
"Set fmDocs = fmApp.Documents" & "¶" &
"For Each fmDoc In fmDocs" & "¶" &
"If InStr(LCase(fmDoc.fullname), LCase(FileName)) > 0 Then" & "¶" &
" fmDoc.dofmscript (ScriptName)" & "¶" &
"End If" & "¶" &
"Next" & "¶" &

"END FUNCTION";

ScriptID = MBS("WindowsScript.Create");
lang= MBS("WindowsScript.SetLanguage"; ScriptID; "VBScript");
addcode = MBS("WindowsScript.AddCode"; ScriptID; VBScriptText)

];
Case(
Get ( SystemPlatform ) = 1;

// Mac OSX Case
MBS( 
"Applescript.Run";
Applescript
);

// Windows Case
MBS("WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction";ScriptID; "DoFMScript";theFileName; theScriptName) & 
MBS("WindowsScript.Close"; ScriptID)

)
)</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

    <Value>

[
JScriptText = "function TestDate(){var d =new Date();
return d.toGMTString();}";
scriptID = MBS("WindowsScript.Create");
SetLang = MBS("WindowsScript.SetLanguage";1;"JScript");
addcode = MBS("WindowsScript.AddCode";1;JScriptText);
theDate = MBS("WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction";1;"TestDate");
closeScript = MBS("WindowsScript.Close";1)

];

theDate

)

</Calculation>
    </Value>
    <Repetition>
      <Calculation>1</Calculation>
    </Repetition>
    <Name>$r</Name>
  </Step>
</fmxmlsnippet>

functions.name: WindowsScript.Create
functions.description: Creates a Windows Script in memory. Once the script is created you can add script code to by using the WindowsScript.AddCode function. The script is then executed using WindowScript.Run. The default language of a new script is Jscript. This can be changed by using the WindowsScript.SetLanguage function. ScriptControl from Microsoft is 32-bit only. If you need 64-bit, please install a 64-bit alternative MSScript.ocx file.

functions.name: WindowsScript.ErrorClear
functions.description: Clears the old errors

functions.name: WindowsScript.ErrorColumn
functions.description: Returns the error column of the last error. For example: If the script encountered an Error at column 16 of the line indicated by the ErrorLine function, "16" would be returned.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.ErrorDescription
functions.description: Returns the text description of the error. This function is free to use. Created 18th August 2014 , last changed 18th August 2014 WindowsScript.ErrorColumn - WindowsScript.ErrorLine 💬 Ask a question or report a problem

functions.name: WindowsScript.ErrorLine
functions.description: Returns the error line of the last error. For example: "1" would be returned id the script encountered an error on the first line.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.ErrorNumber
functions.description: Returns the number of the last error in a VB script.

functions.name: WindowsScript.ErrorSource
functions.description: This function returns the name of the module that had the error. For example: "Runtime error in Microsoft.JScript"

functions.name: WindowsScript.ErrorText
functions.description: Returns the Line of the Script that contains the error. For ex. "FUNCTION myFunction(string" This line of a script is missing a ")" at the end of the line.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.Eval
functions.description: Evaluates the "text" and returns the result.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

parameters.name: Text
parameters.description: The text to evaluate

functions.name: WindowsScript.ExecuteFunction
functions.description: Runs the script specified by the Script ID. This function takes variable number of parameters. Pass as much parameters as needed separated by the semicolon in FileMaker. Please repeat Function Parameter parameter as often as you need.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

parameters.name: FunctionName
parameters.description: The name of the function you are using in the script

parameters.name: Function Parameter...
parameters.description: The first function parameter

functions.name: WindowsScript.ExecuteStatement
functions.description: Executes a statement.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

parameters.name: Script
parameters.description: The script command.

functions.name: WindowsScript.GetAllowUI
functions.description: Returns the Allow UI State of the specified script. Scripts can either allow user interaction or not.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.GetLanguage
functions.description: Queries the current language. The Windows Scripting Host can run scripts that are written in any of several languages. This function returns the current language setting, for example "JScript" or "VBScript"

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.GetTimeout
functions.description: Each script has a setting that dictates how long a script will wait before timing out. This function returns that setting.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.GetUseSafeSubset
functions.description: Returns the Safe Subset State of the specified script. If Use Safe Subset is enabled then subset of the normal functions are allowed.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.SetAllowUI
functions.description: Sets the "Allow UI State of the specified script. Scripts can either allow user interaction or not.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

functions.name: WindowsScript.SetLanguage
functions.description: Returns the Line of the Script that contains the error. For ex. "FUNCTION myFunction(string" This line of a script is missing a ")" at the end of the line.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

parameters.name: Language
parameters.description: The scripting language you want to use

functions.name: WindowsScript.SetTimeout
functions.description: Each script has a setting that dictates how long a script will wait before timing out. This function allows you to change to change that setting. The units are seconds.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.

parameters.name: TimeOut
parameters.description: Seconds, default is 10000

functions.name: WindowsScript.SetUseSafeSubset
functions.description: Sets the Safe Subset State of the specified script. If Use Safe Subset is enabled then subset of the normal functions are allowed.

parameters.name: ScriptID
parameters.description: The ID of a WindowsScript in memory.
